There was a time when nobody noticed you.
Nobody called.
Nobody believed in you.
Nobody asked how you were doing.
Nobody saw the battles you fought when the lights went out.
Everybody started as nobody before they became somebody. Because "nobody" isn't where your story ends.
It's where it begins.

title: Porfirio Cadena- El ojo de vidrio
author: System Fail
description:
¡Uff! Ahora sí nos metemos a una de las grandes leyendas de la radio mexicana. Si Chucho el Roto era el caballero elegante, Porfirio Cadena, "El Ojo de Vidrio", era el equivalente norteño de un western mezclado con melodrama, corridos, persecuciones y balazos. Básicamente, un "Red Dead Redemption" antes de que existieran los videojuegos... y con presupuesto de efectos especiales consistente en una puerta azotándose y un señor agitando una lámina para hacer truenos.
¿Quién era Porfirio Cadena?
Porfirio Cadena es un personaje ficticio creado por el escritor regiomontano Rosendo Ocaña. La historia se desarrolla principalmente en el noreste de México, especialmente en la Sierra del Huajuco, entre Nuevo León y Tamaulipas, un escenario perfecto para caballos, cañones, haciendas y venganzas. La frase con la que muchos recuerdan la radionovela era algo parecido a: "Aquí comienza la borrascosa historia de Porfirio Cadena..." Y con eso bastaba para que miles de personas dejaran lo que estaban haciendo.
¿Por qué se hizo criminal?
Esa es justamente la primera gran saga. No nació siendo un bandido. Era un joven trabajador cuya vida cambia cuando varios hombres poderosos asesinan a sus padres. Durante el ataque intenta defenderlos y pierde un ojo, por lo que termina usando un ojo de vidrio, origen de su famoso apodo. Sin encontrar justicia, decide buscar venganza por su cuenta, iniciando una vida como fugitivo perseguido por las autoridades y por quienes destruyeron su familia. Eso hacía muy interesante al personaje. No era un villano. Era un hombre empujado por las circunstancias. Un antihéroe Lo fascinante de Porfirio es que constantemente está en esa línea donde el público se pregunta:
¿Es un criminal?
Sí. ¿Es un asesino?
 También.
 ¿Es buena persona?
...depende del capítulo. Ayudaba a campesinos. Protegía mujeres. Defendía a los pobres. Pero también disparaba primero y preguntaba después.

Digamos que los abogados actuales tendrían mucho trabajo intentando justificarlo.

Tune in for a blend of humor, heartfelt conversations, and a touch of whiskey wisdom. Each episode is a chance to unwind, laugh out loud, and maybe even learn something new about bourbon. So, grab a glass, sit back, and join us on this comedic journey through the ups and downs of dad life, all while enjoying a good pour.

Therapist life: burnout, imposter syndrome, boundaries & self-care
Clinical education and tools for effective, ethical practice
Supervision, mentorship & therapist identity

Private practice tips & building a sustainable career

title: Skin State of Mind
author: Eva Johnston
description:
Today we know, that isolated resolution of skin problems or aging does not exist. Miracle creams have not yet been invented. If we want to achieve a natural long lasting skin youth, we have to look at our lifestyle as a whole. We need to take into account not only dry scientific facts supported by evidence based medical practice, with maximum safety and precautions, but also we need to listen to our gut feeling. Where we are from,  our genetic history as much as what we drink, eat and breathe are equally important.

In my podcast I will share the latest scientific insights on skin longevity, and together, we will dig deep into the history of skin science,  revisit skin regeneration and particularly, the powerful interdependence between our skin and mental health. The goal of this podcast is to provide you with verified scientific information so that you can make informed decisions about your skin health. Let’s look at it as your skin news channel.

description:
A podcast dedicated to exploring the parts of European football most never see. Beyond the bright lights of the Champions League, we cover the world of Europe's third tier competition, the Conference League. Join us for matchday reviews, deep dives, and the latest stories from around the Conference.
Conference of Dreams is an independent podcast and is not affiliated with UEFA or any football club.

title: No grit, no pearls. - by Oyster Bay
author: Felix Leonhardt
description:
No grit, no pearls. Real talk on food, venture capital, and building what matters.
Every episode: unfiltered answers to the questions founders and investors actually ask - about raising capital, building in food & agtech, and what it really takes to get from idea to exit. Answered by your Host Felix Leonhardt, who made the jump from founder to investor at Oyster Bay himself.
Oyster Bay Venture Capital is one of Europe's first dedicated Food & AgTech VCs, investing in impact-driven founders across the entire food value chain. The portfolio includes major brands like Oatly, Air Up, and La Vie.

title: Rewatch Culture Pod
author: Shanaye
description: Rewatch Culture is a space for revisiting the shows we can't stop rewatching. Hosted by Shanaye, each episode dives into the comfort shows that shaped us, raised us, and maybe pissed us off lol. Pull up a seat and stay awhile. New Episodes out every Sunday.

title: Solutions From Ukraine
author: Rubryka Media Outlet
description:
The Solutions from Ukraine podcast, from the Rubryka media outlet, presents exclusive stories on Ukraine amid the full-scale war that Russia launched on February 22, 2022. 
Hosts: Anastasia Rudenko, editor-in-chief of the independent all-Ukrainian Rubryka media outlet and Vladyslav Faraponov, an analyst at Internews Ukraine NGO. 
Every week they cover a story of Ukraine’s resistance amid the war and explore solutions that communities in Ukraine develop despite the horrors of war. 
Read us at: https://rubryka.com/en/
Support us at: https://www.patreon.com/rubryka

title: Navigating Companion Animals' Digestive Health
author: Companion Consultancy
description: In this podcast, Fiona Farmer discusses navigating companion animals' digestive health with guest hosts Ali McGrath and Carolina Cunha.

title: Better Than I Found You
author: Megan Darrell
description: One friend, one annoying part of adulting pulled from a jar — live. One goal: leave you better than I found you. New episodes Thursdays.

title: Trust The Process Podcast
author: Trust The Process Podcast
description: 🎙️ The podcast everyone's about to be talking about. Unfiltered conversations, bold opinions, and zero filters. Single. Sexy. Grown AF. Trust The Process.

title: Auburn on Main
author: City of Auburn
description: It's fun, it's exciting, it's educational and it's maybe a little decadent. Join hosts Jonathan Glover and Chester Boyd as they deliver the things every Auburn resident should know about.

title: Can We Just Get a Minute?
author: Can We Just Get a Minute?
description: The podcast where we talk honestly about motherhood, life, divorce, dating and everything in between.

title: GoDunzo
author: Forrest Schwartz
description: We’re not just marketers, we own electrical companies too. We know the frustrations: seasonal slowdowns, unreliable marketing agencies who don’t understand the trade, and leads that go cold because follow-up falls through the cracks. We’ve bought and scaled our own electrical businesses, taking them from scratch to consistent 7 figure revenue. That’s why we built this agency: systems that worked for our companies first, now proven for contractors like you.

title: Ordinary Women
author: Rebekah Ricks
description: Ordinary Women is a Christian homeschool mom podcast about faith, family, and everyday courage, hosted by Rebekah Ricks — homeschool mom, former teacher, and legislative strategist with 40+ bills passed. If you’ve ever whispered ”Am I ruining my kids?” over cold coffee, this show is for you. In short 10-minute episodes, Rebekah tackles the questions every homeschooling family faces: Am I qualified to teach my own children? What about socialization? What does homeschooling really cost? What are my parental rights and the homeschool laws in my state? And how do I disciple my kids at the kitchen table without losing my mind? Homeschooled herself, founder of The Homeschool Connection, and author of Kitchen Table Revolution: Why Homeschooling Is the Most Radical Thing You’ll Ever Do, Rebekah brings real data, real stories, and real Scripture — with zero guilt and way too much coffee. New episodes every Tuesday and Thursday. A Courage & Caffeine production. You’re not crazy. You’re called. Pull up a chair.

title: Goldenrod - A Horror Podcast
author: Lauren Tucker, Speak to It Productions
description:
Burdened with a creative block, troubled young writer Joan Cassidy finds escape at Twig Lake Resort. Yet, as the days creep by, Joan finds that their newfound escape might be a novel nightmare. One thing is clear: something is haunting Joan Cassidy, and they must determine if there is really any escaping that which lurks beyond the treeline, breathless to consume. 
Equal parts snug, sinister, and blisteringly emotional, Goldenrod is a horror-drama scripted fiction podcast, airing weekly in eight parts. 
For mature listeners. Please check episode content warnings. Headphones recommended.

title: GENTALK
author: Gentalk
description: A youth podcast that help young people come all together to express their point of views on deferents topics

title: Love and the Law
author: pjcampanarolaw
description: Join attorney P.J. Campanaro and other legal experts as they discuss family law and how it affects everyday lives and intersects with many other areas of the law.

title: Texas Industrial Energy Efficiency Program (TIEEP)
author: UHEnergy
description:
Learn more about the University of Houston’s outreach program and various events on energy and water management for Texas industries. This podcast is our newest endeavor to meaningfully engage with engineers, managers, and consultants working across the process industries.
The podcast is produced by UH Energy at the UH Media Lab with the support of the State Energy Conservation Office (SECO) in Austin, Texas. Every fortnight we look ahead to interesting industrial energy events in Houston or online, talk about interesting articles we have read and talks we’ve heard, and feature a new article

title: Torch Talk: A Smallville Rewatch Podcast
author: Jeremiah and Myriah
description: Grab your red jacket and watch out for meteor rocks. Torch Talk is a weekly "Smallville" rewatch podcast hosted by longtime friends Jeremiah and Myriah. Join in as they revisit every episode of "Smallville," one week at a time. Whether you watched it when it aired or are discovering it for the first time, follow along as they break down Clark Kent’s early hero moments, the drama, villains, friendships, romances, and early 2000s nostalgia while unpacking what still works, laughing at what feels wonderfully dated, and celebrating why the show still has a loyal fanbase.

title: Next Level, with Nic
author: Nicole
description:
You've built something real. The business, the family, the life that looks incredible from the outside.

However, privately? You are exhausted, disconnected, running on empty and confused as to why it doesn't feel the way it was supposed to.
NEXT LEVEL, with Nic is for that woman, the woman who is done wondering and ready to actually take action.
Every week Nicole Abbott, High Performance Health Coach, NLP Prac, Surfer and Mumma of three brings you REAL talk, honest conversations and the practical tools that actually move the needle.
Health, Identity, Habits & Life design. No fluff & No filters. 
Because you, Sis, YOU deserve to feel as good as your life looks.
New episodes every Tuesday.

title: Contractor Cents
author: Ruth King
description: Discover ideas, tactics, news, and information that matters to your contracting business and you. Increase productivity, profitability, customer and employee satisfaction.

title: Comunicación eficaz
author: Melissa
description: Bienvenidos a este podcast educativo sobre "La importancia de la comunicación eficaz"

title: What the Money
author: Dr. CC Anthony
description:
Welcome to the ROOTED™ What the Money Podcast
Financial intelligence, ownership thinking, and generational wealth through real-world education and conversation.
Hosted by Dr. CC Anthony, AAMS™ — Financial Advisor, Professor, Author, and Speaker — ROOTED™ delivers practical insight to help individuals and families better understand money, business, and long-term wealth building.
Important Disclosures can be Found here: www.drccanthony.finance

title: Jump Around
author: Mitchell Ellis
description:
Jump Around brings you in depth racing analysis with a healthy dose of banter and good fun. From breaking down the form to sharing a few laughs along the way, the team will be covering the 4 remaining feature jumps meetings in Victoria. 
Join us as we look to grow the focus around the jumps races as well as deep dive into niche areas of the racing community.

title: Seasoned and Seen
author: LaTara Venise
description:
Seasoned & Seen™ Podcast is a lifestyle-centered conversation for women 50+ who understand that life isn’t winding down… it’s deepening.

Hosted by Dr. LaTara Venise, this podcast creates space for honest, soulful conversations around identity, relationships, wellness, purpose, and legacy. Through real stories and lived experiences, each episode highlights the beauty, strength, and wisdom that come with this season of life.

This isn’t a place for polished performances or surface-level talk.
This is where women come to be seen, heard, and reminded of who they are becoming.

No pressure. No pretending.
Just real conversation, connection, and becoming.

title: Mastering An Post Tracking
author: Emily
description: Explore how An Post Tracking streamlines the process of managing your parcels, registered letters, and international shipments. This episode covers everything from tracking numbers to delivery statuses, ensuring you stay informed every step of the way. Discover the features of An Post's free online service and app, including real-time updates and customer support options, making parcel tracking convenient and efficient

title: Talk Pretty 2 Me
author: Madison and Gracie Shortt
description:
🎙️Two sisters. One Mic. No filter. 
🥂Life, laughs, and lessons from our 20s!
Follow us on Instagram @talk.prettytome

title: Real Estate News: Real Estate Investing Podcast
author: Kathy Fettke / RealWealth
description: Don't get caught off guard by market crashes that can take all your money down with them. And don't miss out on markets where you can build wealth practically overnight. Real Estate News for Investors with Kathy Fettke is the premiere source for savvy real estate investors who want to stay up-to-date on new laws, regulations, and economic events that affect real estate. Topics include: market trends, economic analysis that affects housing prices, updates on the best rental markets for investing in single-family rentals or multi-unit rentals, turn-key housing standards, the fate of the highly revered 1031 exchange and other tax law affecting investors, self-directed IRA investing and 401k changes, where rents and property values are rising or falling, flipping risks, new Dodd-Frank rules regarding private lending and financing standards, areas with job losses vs job growth, areas that are overbuilt or over-supplied versus areas with low supply and high demand, and how to avoid real esta...

title: The Room in the Dragon Volant: A Gothic Mystery
author: Joseph Sheridan Le Fanu
description:
Richard Beckett's heart races when he catches a fleeting glimpse of a veiled Countess in a crowded French square, her presence igniting a dangerous obsession. Seeking to uncover the mystery behind her haunting beauty, he secures a room at the Dragon Volant, a notorious inn shrouded in whispers of terror and unsolved disappearances. As Richard delves deeper into the secrets of the inn and the shadowy figure that captivates him, he finds himself drawn into a web of intrigue, where love and peril intertwine, and every corner holds a clue, or a threat.

In this chilling tale of Gothic horror, Joseph Sheridan Le Fanu weaves a narrative rich with suspense and supernatural elements. What sinister forces lie behind the Countess's veil? Can Richard protect her from the dangers that lurk in the shadows of the Dragon Volant? With each passing moment, the stakes rise, and Richard must confront not only the mysteries of the past but also the darkness within himself.

Join Richard on his harrowing journey as he navigates love, betrayal, and the unknown. Experience the tension of a haunted inn, the allure of forbidden romance, and the spine-tingling suspense that defines this classic supernatural fiction. "Room in the Dragon Volant" will keep you on the edge of your seat, eager to uncover its secrets.

title: Stacking Victories
author: Chad
description:
Stacking Victories focuses on the real-life stories of what God has done in their individual recovery journey. 
These stories are from a multitude of areas from addictions (chemical and non-chemical), attempted suicide, anxiety, depression, divorce, separation in marriage, and so much more. 
We aim to bring experience, strength and hope to those seeking relief and growth in bettering their lives.

title: Becoming UnDone® with Dr. Toby Brooks
author: Dr. Toby Brooks
description: Hosted by professor, speaker, and performance & comeback scientist Dr. Toby Brooks (@drtobybrooks), Becoming UnDone® is where high-achievers come to reckon with rock bottom—and rise again. Every week, we explore what happens when life doesn’t go according to plan. Through raw stories, vulnerable insights, and powerful conversations, we dive into the pain of identity loss, the paralysis of burnout, and the transformative power of coming apart.This isn’t just about failure. It’s about what you do next.Whether you’re an executive questioning your purpose, an athlete facing the end, or a leader secretly unraveling, this show is your reminder: the coming apart isn’t the end of the story—it’s the beginning of something greater. If you’ve ever asked yourself, “What in the hell next?”—you’re in the right place.https://linktr.ee/DrTobyBrooks

title: In The Hot Seat | Playaz Productions Network
author: Playaz Productions Network
description: Welcome to In The Hot Seat, the podcast that skips the small talk and goes straight to the fire. Hosted by the PPN team, this show puts innovators, disruptors, and newsmakers under the microscope. We don't just ask the easy questions—we dig into the decisions, failures, and breakthroughs that define success. Whether you’re looking for raw truth, sharp insights, or just want to see how today’s biggest names handle the pressure, hit subscribe. It’s about to get warm in here.

title: The Unveiling Files
author: Eleven 22 Entertainment
description: The Unveiling Files is a serialized supernatural mystery podcast hosted by investigative journalist Emma Lane. Each season uncovers a new case where folklore, history, and the unexplained collide. Season One: The Black Pyramid follows Emma as she investigates the disappearance of Sheriff Tess Branson after a mysterious woman returns to her small hometown without having aged in twenty years. As Emma digs deeper, she uncovers a chilling mystery that challenges everything she believes about reality.

title: Couch Talk Pod
author: Couch Talk Pod
description: Margarita and Mason dive into the creative wedding industry to give insight of their growing journey in it!

title: Behind a Mask: The Hidden Power of Jean Muir
author: Louisa May Alcott
description:
When Jean Muir steps into the lavish Coventry household as a governess, she captivates everyone with her charm and grace. But beneath her polished exterior lies a web of secrets and manipulations that could unravel the very fabric of the family she serves. In "Behind a Mask, or a Woman's Power," Louisa May Alcott, writing as A. M. Barnard, weaves a tantalizing tale of deception, ambition, and the quest for autonomy in a world that seeks to define a woman's worth.

As Jean navigates her role, she forms a complex relationship with the naïve young heiress, while also setting her sights on the charming but oblivious master of the house. With each calculated move, Jean reveals the stark contrast between societal expectations and her own desires. What unfolds is a gripping exploration of identity and power dynamics, where every interaction teeters on the edge of intrigue.

Listeners will become engrossed in Jean's dual existence as she juggles her public persona and hidden ambitions. Can one woman’s pursuit of freedom and influence change the narrative of her life? Experience the suspense and allure of Alcott’s lesser-known sensation novel, where every character hides behind a mask, and nothing is as it seems. This audiobook invites you to unravel the layers of intrigue, leaving you questioning the true nature of power and the lengths one will go to achieve it.

title: The Curated Culture
author: Rob Boggan
description:
Welcome to The Curated Culture – where pop culture, tech, and society collide! Join me, Robbie Deezle, as we uncover the stories, people, and innovations shaping our ever-evolving world. From thought-provoking interviews to deep dives into the trends that matter, we're all about celebrating the pulse of our diverse culture.
Hit that subscribe button and tune in!

title: Hamlet Protein Podcasts
author: Hamlet Protein
description: Hamlet Protein is a global supplier of specialty soy proteins for young animal nutrition, helping producers improve performance during the most critical stages of animal development. With decades of research and expertise, Hamlet Protein develops highly digestible protein ingredients that support gut health, nutrient utilization and long-term productivity in swine and poultry production. Through science, innovation and close collaboration with customers worldwide, Hamlet Protein is dedicated to advancing animal nutrition and creating value across the feed industry.

title: The Sword of Damocles: Love and Mystery in New York
author: Anna Katharine Green
description:
When a mysterious murder disrupts the lives of New York City's elite, amateur sleuths and lovers become entangled in a web of secrets and deception. In "Sword of Damocles," Anna Katharine Green weaves a gripping tale that explores the intersections of love, ambition, and justice. As the story unfolds, characters grapple with their desires and the consequences of their choices, revealing the fragile nature of trust.

At the center of this intricate plot is the enigmatic figure of Mr. Gryce, a detective whose brief but pivotal appearances add layers of intrigue. But it’s the lives of ordinary people, caught in extraordinary circumstances, that drive the narrative. Each character faces their own conflicts—some striving for love, others for vengeance, and a few simply trying to survive amidst the chaos of a bustling city.

Green’s keen insights into human nature and her pioneering role in detective fiction shine through as she deftly balances romance and mystery. Will the truth behind the murder be unveiled? Can love conquer the shadows of betrayal? Journey through this captivating tale where every twist invites you to question the very fabric of societal norms. "Sword of Damocles" is not just a mystery; it's a reflection on the human condition, perfect for fans of classic detective stories and romantic entanglements. Tune in for a thrilling experience that will keep you guessing until the last moment.

title: All The Waves
author: Visit the Northshore
description: Welcome to All The Waves, a podcast by Visit The Northshore! Join us as we explore the hidden gems, untold stories and the heart of our beloved destination. From local legends to incredible adventures, our podcast promises to be as funny and charming as it is informative.

title: Pastor Dan Broyles | Spiritual Care: Relationship Series
author: Pastor Dan
description: Spiritual Care: Relationship Series is a practical, hope-filled podcast with Pastor Dan Broyles, featuring Dr. Michal Carolyn Wilson, that combines biblical wisdom with clinical insight to help you build healthier relationships, find emotional healing, and navigate life's challenges with confidence and grace.

title: The Green Mummy: A Thrilling Quest for Secrets and Suspense
author: Fergus Hume
description:
Professor Augustus P. Wimple's obsession with a long-lost green mummy takes a deadly turn when his archaeological pursuits unearth more than just ancient artifacts. As he digs through layers of history, he stumbles upon a chilling murder that intertwines his fate with those of his curious acquaintances. What begins as a quest for knowledge spirals into a twisted mystery, forcing Wimple to confront not only the secrets of the past but also the darkness lurking in the present.

Join Wimple and his motley crew, including the sharp-witted journalist Clara Tate and the skeptical detective Samuel Graves, as they navigate a labyrinth of clues, hidden agendas, and unexpected alliances. Each revelation leads them closer to uncovering the truth behind the murder, but with every step, the stakes rise higher. Can Wimple unravel the mystery before it claims more lives?

In 'Green Mummy,' Fergus Hume masterfully blends elements of crime and adventure, drawing listeners into a world where every character has a secret and every discovery could be their last. Fast-paced and filled with twists, this audiobook keeps you guessing until the very end. Dive into a tale of ambition, betrayal, and ancient wonders that will leave you on the edge of your seat. Will the truth be worth the price they must pay?

title: Med Spa Marketing Guys | SEO, AI & Patient Growth Podcast
author: Andrew
description: Discover how modern med spas and aesthetic clinics can generate more booked consultations, reduce no-shows, increase repeat bookings, and build predictable growth. The Med Spa Marketing Guys | SEO, AI & Patient Growth Podcast explores practical strategies around Med Spa Marketing, SEO, Google Ads, Meta Ads, CRM automation, GoHighLevel, AI follow-up, landing pages, review generation, patient reactivation, and marketing systems designed for aesthetic clinics. Learn more at https://medspamarketingguys.com/

title: The Middle East decoded
author: Strife observatory
description: Welcome to the Strife Observatory podcast. Here, we break down conflicts, tensions, and the major geopolitical issues of the Middle East. Through each episode, we help you better understand a complex region at the heart of international affairs.

title: 5 Early Mornings
author: 5FM
description: The memo for 5 Early Mornings is crystal clear: ENERGY. HYPE. VIBES – because South Africa DESERVES to wake up on the best possible note. Featuring the hottest music, the best chats & features, and all the laughs in between, join The Big Chief, Tshepi Moji and make 5 Early Mornings your first port of call to get your mornings started the right way. Missed out on anything? Catch up on the latest that happened on 5 Early Mornings here!

title: Late Nights with Kay
author: Kassandra Fuller
description: The late night podcast that you’ll never know what you’re going to hear. Either way, you’ll enjoy it!

title: Vital Signs
author: Ahmed Ragab
description:
Vital Signs is a podcast that tracks the pulse of progress.
Each week we’ll be sitting down with the authors of timely publications that follow the signpost topics shaping progressive policy and theory.

title: Parenting On The Spectrum
author: ParentingOnTheSpectrum
description: Showing what austim awareness is really about. Awareness isn’t just about statistics and pin pointing who has autism it’s about the story the struggle the good the bad and ugly side of autism.

title: Cuff Unplugged
author: Cheryl Moses
description:
"Cuff Unplugged" is a podcast featuring interviews with music artists and influencers! It conveys a sense of authenticity, intimacy, and a stripped-down approach to conversations.

Join us for an unfiltered and intimate experience in 'Cuff Unplugged.' We go beyond the spotlight to have raw and authentic conversations with the most compelling music artists and influential figures of our time. From rising stars to industry icons, we sit down with our guests to explore their personal journeys, creative process, and the stories behind their success. Get ready for in-depth interviews, exclusive insights, and the unfiltered truth straight from the hearts and minds of our esteemed guests.

title: Next Level Business
author: Jeni Cohen
description:
🚀 Next Level Business Podcast
Regular discussions on business, the entrepreneurial journey, marketing, sales, branding, and practical strategies for success. Here, you’ll find stories of successful entrepreneurs, actionable business tips, fresh ideas, and insights that can take your business to the next level.
🎙️ New episodes every week
💡 Business Growth | Marketing | Sales | Entrepreneurship | Mindset

title: Lay and Li Know It All
author: Ashley
description: Welcome to a lesson in pop-culture that only two kids could give.  Come listen to sisters Lay and Li break down everything from music icons to wrestling legends and everything in between from a pre-teen POV.

title: One Thing with Pastor CT Townsend & Friends
author: CT Townsend Ministries
description:
The One Thing Podcast with Pastor CT Townsend and Friends offers practical insight and spiritual encouragement to individuals who seek to honor God with their lives.

Join as as we discuss life, leadership, and the Lord's Work one thing at a time. Our show's title comes from Psalm 27:4.

Psalm 27:4 - One thing have I desired of the Lord, that will I seek after; that I may dwell in the house of the Lord all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the Lord, and to inquire in his temple.

Connect with Pastor CT Townsend and his Ministries at https://cttownsend.com/

title: Love You To Pieces
author: Kristiana Morell
description: Conversations that help you come home to yourself.

title: White People Work
author: Scott Hall
description: White people work is a podcast designed to help white folks come to better understand themselves and grow in taking responsibility for their presence in the world. While white folks and their growth in awareness and responsibility is the primary focus, folks of color are encouraged to be a part of this journey and speak into it. Season one will have ten episodes, each with Scott answering a different question related to race and whiteness. Music is performed, recorded, and mixed by Scott Hall, mastered by Erin Hamilton.

title: An-Nadhm Al-Bayyin | Ustadh Imran Duka
author: PMI
description:
An-Nadhm Al-Bayyin: A 50-Line Introductory Poem to Ḥanbalī Fiqh
Course Content:
https://www.scribd.com/document/901124985/An-Nadhm-Al-Bayyin-English

title: Essential Rebellion
author: Cindy Lombard
description:
The Essential Rebellion is a podcast for women who are tired of being told, “That’s just part of aging.”


Hosted by Cindy Lombard, board-certified women’s health nurse practitioner and founder of Essential Woman, this podcast challenges the status quo of women’s healthcare and empowers women to ask better questions, find real answers, and take ownership of their health and quality of life.


From hormones, perimenopause, menopause, metabolic health, and sexual wellness to longevity, sleep, inflammation, peptides, and modern treatments, The Essential Rebellion explores topics that impact how women feel, function, and thrive.


This is not about fighting aging. It’s about aging well.


Join Cindy for honest conversations, evidence-based education, and practical insights to help women stop settling for “normal” and start defining what health, vitality, and quality of life mean for themselves.


Because feeling invisible is not normal. Being dismissed is not acceptable. And your health deserves more than the status quo.


Welcome to The Essential Rebellion.

title: Lawyer Lee Case Files
author: Lee Wallace
description: Lawyer Lee brings you compelling true crime stories, in-depth trial coverage, and clear legal analysis from a veteran attorney. Whether we're unraveling a notorious case from the past or following today's biggest courtroom battles, you'll get the facts, the strategy, and the story behind the headlines.

title: The Pitch Podcast
author: thepitchpodcast
description:
We interview real entrepreneurs, business owners, and career creatives with skin the game. 

We want to hear people's stories from every level of business.

The exited founder who sold for $100M+,
The scrappy start up CEO who is doing $5 million in revenue
The student who just started their AI start up, cleaning company, or creative agency. 

This is the place to come for inspiration, cutting edge industry information, and to hear about the journey to success... not just about the destination. 

This is The Pitch Podcast.

title: The Mark of the Monster: A Tale of Love and Horror
author: Jack Williamson
description:
When the ancient black altar atop the hill begins to stir with dark energy, Alice and Tom find themselves at the center of an unspeakable horror. In "Mark of the Monster," Jack Williamson weaves a gripping tale of love overshadowed by an ominous force that threatens to tear them apart. As they uncover the sinister history of the altar, they confront not only external dangers but also the haunting shadows of their own pasts.

With each revelation, the line between love and fear blurs, leading them into a chilling mystery that tests their bond. As they delve deeper into the altar's dark secrets, they must navigate a web of supernatural occurrences that challenge their understanding of reality. Will their love withstand the monstrous forces lurking in the shadows, or will the doom that looms over them claim them both?

This audiobook invites listeners into an eerie world of suspense, where every whisper might unveil a hidden truth and every turn could lead to disaster. Experience the thrill of discovery and the terror of the unknown as Williamson crafts an unforgettable journey filled with dread, passion, and the supernatural. "Mark of the Monster" isn’t just a tale of horror; it’s an exploration of the depths of love in the face of overwhelming darkness. Tune in and prepare for a heart-pounding experience that will keep you on the edge of your seat.

title: The Comet Doom: A Race Against Cosmic Catastrophe
author: Edmond Hamilton
description:
When a rogue comet hurtles toward Earth, Dr. Michael Thorne races against time to unlock the cosmic mystery threatening humanity's existence. In "The Comet Doom," Edmond Hamilton crafts a gripping tale of science, intrigue, and survival. As the clock ticks down, Thorne uncovers a colossal conspiracy that spans the galaxy, revealing shocking truths about the forces at play in the universe.

This audiobook takes you on a thrilling journey filled with unexpected twists, where every chapter deepens the stakes and challenges our understanding of fate. How far will Thorne go to save his loved ones and the planet? What secrets lie in the depths of space, waiting to be revealed? The exploration of destiny versus free will plays a central role, prompting listeners to ponder profound questions about their own lives.

Join Thorne and a diverse cast of characters as they confront not only the imminent disaster but also their personal demons. The tension escalates with every revelation, immersing you in a world where the line between science and the unknown blurs. Perfect for fans of classic science fiction, "Comet Doom" will keep you on the edge of your seat, eager to discover if humanity can outsmart its fate—or if doom is truly inevitable. Tune in to experience a narrative that blends suspense, wonder, and the relentless pursuit of truth in the face of cosmic catastrophe.

title: Bedtime Myths - Mythology Stories for Kids
author: Bedtime Myths
description: Magical bedtime stories from the world's oldest myths, told in a warm, gentle voice for little ones aged 3-5. Travel to Mount Olympus, sail with Viking gods, and meet magical creatures, all tucked up safe and cosy. New stories every weekday. Sweet dreams, little one.

title: Your Expert Guest
author: Julie Fry
description: Learn how to grow your visibility and reach your ideal clients as a podcast guest. You'll learn how to create interview topics, find the right fit podcasts to pitch and increase the likelihood of getting invited onto podcasts as a guest. Tips for interviews and repurposing your content will be covered too!

title: OnTwelve: Embodied Living
author: Embodied On Twelve
description:
Embodied healing practices: yoga, breathwork, meditation
Energy healing and Reiki wisdom
The beauty and bravery of living with authenticity
Self-love, body acceptance, and building a new relationship with yourself
Life with autoimmune conditions, healing with compassion
Rituals, reflection, and the small sacred moments that change everything
This is not a space for perfection. This is a space for love, truth, and embodiment.
Love — for yourself, for your body, for your journey. Truth — your truth, without shame or apology. Embodiment — learning to live inside yourself with peace and presence.

title: A Really Nice Time With Friends
author: Cyclepath Medicine Hat
description: Cole, Dan & Lance sit down to discuss bikes, shop stories and whatever is on our minds. From our CyclePath Medicine Hat Location we bring you the ramblings of a few bike nerds and their friends, join us!

title: Detour: A Jazz Fiddler's Desperate Journey
author: Martin M. Goldsmith
description:
After losing his job and his girlfriend, jazz fiddle player Alexander Roth faces a crossroads that will change everything. With Sue chasing her Hollywood dreams, Alexander decides to sell his belongings and hit the road to find her. But his journey quickly turns into a desperate scramble for survival when he finds himself stranded and hungry on the side of a lonely highway in California.

As he thumbs for a ride, Alexander's world spins into a whirlwind of unexpected encounters, each person he meets holds a piece of the mystery that is his own life. From a quirky hitchhiker with a secret to a wise old musician who knows more than he lets on, every interaction forces Alexander to confront his past choices and his future.

In 'Detour,' the lines between hope and despair blur as Alexander navigates through the twists and turns of fate. Will he find Sue before it's too late? And what price will he pay for love and redemption? This gripping tale weaves themes of ambition, heartbreak, and the pursuit of dreams against the backdrop of America's highways. Tune in to experience a poignant story about second chances and the surprising paths we take in life.

Join Alexander on his journey, where every mile unveils a new layer of intrigue and emotion. 'Detour' is not just a quest for love; it's a deep dive into the soul of a man searching for his place in a world that seems to have forgotten him.

title: Chronic Conundrums
author: The Sydney Children’s Hospitals Network
description:
Created by four young people living with chronic illness and/or disability to create a space for conversations about their realities.
Hosted by Joel Brookes and Lani Puse, with writing and research by Charlotte Mckenzie and Charlie Moten, the podcast explores a range of experiences from diagnoses and hospitalisations to treatment and health care transition services.
Chronic Conundrums is endorsed by the Sydney Children’s Hospitals Network. This podcast is not intended to be a substitute for professional mental health advice. If you find any topics explored potentially triggering, please seek help from a local mental health professional or contact Kids Helpline on 1800 55 1800 for 24-hour advice.

title: The Return of Tarzan (A New Adventure Begins)
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
description:
Tarzan's journey takes an unexpected turn when he leaves America, heartbroken after sacrificing his love for Jane Porter. As he boards a ship to Europe, he encounters the enigmatic Countess Olga de Coude, whose life is threatened by the sinister schemes of Nikolas Rokoff and his ruthless henchman, Alexis Paulvitch. In a world that feels foreign and disconnected, Tarzan must navigate treachery and deception while forging new alliances.

This thrilling sequel to "Tarzan of the Apes" plunges listeners into a whirlwind of action and intrigue. With each twist, Tarzan faces not only external foes but also the internal struggle of belonging and identity. As he confronts betrayal and danger, he discovers that the jungle's laws may not apply to the sophisticated social circles of Europe.

Listeners will be on the edge of their seats as Tarzan's legendary strength and primal instincts clash with the cunning of his adversaries. Will he protect the innocent and reclaim his place in the world, or will he fall victim to the dark plots surrounding him?

"The Return of Tarzan" invites you to embark on an exhilarating adventure filled with mystery, romance, and the relentless quest for redemption. Join Tarzan as he battles not just for survival, but for the very essence of who he is, making this a must-listen for fans of action and adventure!

title: My Business On Purpose
author: Scott Beebe
description: We coach small business owners to uncover the things they cannot see, and implement systems and processes that help them live their business on purpose

title: 2guys_1movie
author: Floppy & Kiel
description: We are just two guys that love to watch movies and talk about them... Movies that are good, movies that are bad, and movies that are fucking rad!

title: The Small Town Social Workers
author: Nathanael Ferguson & Kat Maynard
description: The Small Town Social Worker's Podcast, hosted by Nathanael Ferguson, LMSW and Kathleen Maynard, MSW, dives into crucial topics within the Child Welfare System. Through a blend of professional insights, personal experiences, and practical knowledge, the podcast offers invaluable support to individuals and families navigating topics within the Child Welfare system. Each episode tackles issues such as advocacy, resources, and challenges faced by the hosts, as social workers and as parents, aiming to empower listeners with information and strategies for positive change.

title: BOSS Business of Surgery Series
author: Amy Vertrees, MD
description:
Welcome to BOSS Business of Surgery Series! 
This program was specifically designed to help surgeons learn concepts not taught in residency but necessary for a successful surgery career. 
We were not told that most of our job would be interacting with others. We thought it was about the technical success of surgery or the knowledge that we learn. 
But it is so much more.
Difficult partners and colleagues. 
Dealing with complications. 
Negotiating with administration. 
Running a successful and efficient clinic that doesn’t take bleed into our home life. 
How to have a life outside of surgery
But if we don’t learn these concepts, we will end up in a negative spiral that will lead us into misery. And all of the time we spent training for the job we love, that could be so rewarding, is lost. 
You know there has to be a solution out there. That you can’t be the only one unhappy or wondering if it is just you. 
It’s time for a program that addresses your specific problems run by someone who knows what you are going through. You need a fellow surgeon who knows the way. You need a surgeon who has been where you are and found her way out to the other side:
-Loving surgery again
-Not taking work home
-finishing notes immediately after clinic and heading home on time
-Not letting complications set you back
-Interacting with others with confidence
-Finally seeing that you can control the results you get at work and home
You can find out more about Dr. Vertrees and her work at www.BOSSsurgery.com.

title: Five Children and It (A Magical Adventure with Wishes)
author: E. Nesbit
description:
When Cyril, Anthea, Robert, and Jane stumble upon a mysterious Sand-Fairy in an old gravel pit, their lives take a whimsical turn. This ancient creature, known as the Psammead, grants them one wish each day, leading to a series of fantastical adventures that promise delight and chaos. But with every wish, the children quickly learn that magic comes with its own set of challenges. From transforming into animals to experiencing the world from a new perspective, their desires often lead to unexpected consequences.

As the siblings navigate their newfound powers, they grapple with the moral dilemmas of wish-making, the importance of family, and the value of appreciating what they already have. Each wish reveals more than just magical possibilities; it uncovers deeper truths about friendship, responsibility, and the complexities of growing up.

Join Cyril, Anthea, Robert, and Jane on a journey filled with laughter, lessons, and the enchanting unpredictability of their Sand-Fairy's gifts. Will they master the art of wishing, or will their fantasies spiral out of control? Dive into 'Five Children and It' and discover the joy and peril that come with every magical wish. Perfect for listeners of all ages, this classic tale invites you to ponder—what would you wish for?

title: Benighted: A Stormy Night of Suspense and Intrigue
author: John Boynton Priestley
description:
When Phillip and Margaret Waverton's car skids off the road during a relentless storm, they stumble upon an eerie, isolated house that seems to hide more than just its creaky floors and dark corners. This old mansion is home to the peculiar Femms, whose oddities turn a simple shelter into a night of suspense and revelation. As the storm rages outside, the Wavertons and their spirited friend Roger Penderel quickly realize that they are not merely guests but unwitting participants in a chilling mystery.

Behind the facade of hospitality lies a tangled web of secrets, where every creak of the floorboards and whispered conversation hints at deeper, more sinister truths. With the unsettling presence of the mute butler Morgan lurking in the shadows, tensions rise as strange occurrences lead the trio to question who—or what—they can trust.

‘Benighted’ weaves a gripping tale of psychological intrigue and moral ambiguity, challenging listeners to navigate the delicate line between reality and illusion. As the clock ticks and the storm continues to howl, the Wavertons must confront their own fears and unravel the enigma of the Femms before it’s too late. Will they escape the night unscathed, or will the secrets of the house consume them? Dive into this captivating audiobook to experience a masterclass in suspense that explores the darker sides of human nature.

title: Chills Thrills And A Podcast
author: Reggie Simpkins
description:
A Horror Community Podcast Where I Review And Discuss Any And All Things Horror.

Follow The Show On Instagram & TikTok


Inquiries and/or collabs

title: Echoes in the Dark with Rae Wilson
author: Rae
description:
In the oral tradition of storytelling, Echoes in the Dark, delivers classic works of gothic fiction weekly. Hosted and curated by Ms. Rae (an award-winning educator, actor, and literary analyst) the collection of stories spans popular works by authors like Edgar Allan Poe  as well as lesser known works by authors such as Guy de Maupassant. Each story is followed by a literary analysis. 


If you’re looking to enjoy more classic literature, struggle to find the time to read, hate reading, or just love listening to stories, then this podcast is for you. 


A Note on Content: While these stories are generally appropriate for listeners aged 12 and up, classic Gothic literature frequently explores themes of murder, romantic affairs, and "tortured souls." Stories are performed exactly as written in their original historical context. 


If you are looking to learn more ways to boost your literary analysis visit:

title: Infinite Talent
author: Ian Wilkinson
description: Infinite Talent celebrates the power of age diversity in the workplace. We explore how to break free from age stereotypes, spotlight the value of multi-generational teams, and share actionable insights to attract and retain talent across all age groups. Featuring real-life success stories and expert advice, this podcast empowers HR professionals and business leaders to build inclusive workplaces where every generation thrives. Infinite Talent: Because brilliance knows no age.

title: Get Real Rae... Real Business & Life Advice
author: Rae Wilson
description: Being a Solopreneur is hard. Life is hard. And if you can hardly stand another podcast where the host makes everything sound easy breezy, another interview where it seems like everyone except you  is making millions, then this Podcast is for you.

description: Welcome, let's have a chat. If you've ever had a chance to chat with The Luvely Rae at one of her New York City shows, then you may recall that she is always more than happy to discuss the little things, and as always to hear your thoughts. In this podcast The Luvely Rae (a rather lovely intellectual ecdysiast) invites you stay awhile for a brief conversation on the cultures and cultural changes. The thought provoking topics are meant to be conversation starters, and are provided akin to the popular weather girls of 1955 and the stimulating interludes of Gypsy Rose Lee. If there's a topic you'd like to chat about, feel free connect.

title: Texas Property Tax Strategies
author: texaspropertytaxstrategies
description: Texas Property Tax Strategies is a podcast dedicated to helping property owners in Texas navigate the complexities of property tax assessments and disputes.The show features expert insights from attorneys and industry professionals who provide practical advice and strategies for managing property tax issues. Whether you're a commercial property owner or a real estate investor, this podcast offers valuable information to help you understand your rights and options in the Texas property tax landscape.

title: Sea and the Jungle
author: Henry Major Tomlinson
description: Considered one of the greatest travel narratives, The Sea and the Jungle is H.M. Tomlinson's firsthand account of the first journey of an English steamer up the Madeira in Brazil. Bored with his unfulfilling job in London, Tomlinson boards the steamer "Capella" at Swansea and embarks on a journey to deliver supplies to the men building railroads for the rubber harvest. The book overflows with vivid descriptions of the sea voyage and the tropics, as well as Tomlinson's dry sense of humor and observations. In his time in the Amazon and at sea, Tomlinson encounters storms, rotted food, yellow fever, angry mules, rapids, mosquitoes, and the foreboding character of the Amazon rainforest. (Summary by Tatiana Chichilla)

title: A Nostalgic Education
author: Jennifer Foley
description: A podcast all about the nostalgia of the 90s.

description: Choosing your medical specialty could make or break your career. The Rotation is a matchmaking show that digs into the realities of different doctors to help you discover your path.

title: Pretty and Problematic
author: Selena DaCosta
description:
Welcome to Pretty & Problematic 🎙️💕
I’m Selena, and every week we’re having the conversations people usually avoid. Relationships, heartbreak, healing, motherhood, trauma, self worth, rebuilding your life, and figuring out who you are after everything you’ve been through.
I don’t have all the answers, and I’m not here to pretend I do. I’m just sharing my experiences, the lessons I’ve learned, and the conversations I wish someone had with me years ago.
If you’ve ever felt unseen, unchosen, or like you had to earn love, I hope this podcast reminds you that you’re not alone and that healing is possible ✨

title: Unfiltered Faith
author: NBCY
description: Unfiltered Faith is where real conversations meet real faith. Join students, local pastors, and influential voices as we unpack today's biggest questions, explore what the Bible actually says, and dive deeper into Christianity than we ever could on a Sunday evening.

title: P91 Podcast
author: Jessica Norman
description:
Hey, friend! Welcome to P91. I'm Jessica, and I'm really glad you're here.

If you're looking for someone who has life all figured out... this probably isn't the podcast for you. (And if you find that person, let me know because I've got a few questions.)

Around here, we're just going to have real conversations. We'll laugh a little, probably cry a little, and talk about what it looks like to follow God in the middle of everyday life—the good days, the messy days, and the "Lord, what are You doing?" kind of days.

My prayer is that every time you listen, you leave feeling a little lighter, a little stronger, and reminded that God hasn't forgotten about you.

So grab your coffee, your sweet tea, or whatever gets you through the day, get comfortable, and let's have a conversation.

This is P91—real people, real faith, and a God who's faithful through it all.

Let's get into it.

Through practical coaching strategies, military-inspired principles, and proven mindset training, we equip coaches, parents, and athletes to work together around one standard—creating stronger team cultures, resilient competitors, and leaders who reach their full potential both on and off the field.

title: Personal Narrative of a Pilgrimage to Al-madinah and Meccah
author: Richard Francis Burton
description: Sir Richard Francis Burton (1821 – 1890) was an English explorer, translator, writer, soldier, orientalist, ethnologist, linguist, poet, hypnotist, fencer and diplomat. He was known for his travels and explorations within Asia and Africa as well as his extraordinary knowledge of languages and cultures. According to one count, he spoke 29 European, Asian, and African languages.Burton's best-known achievements include traveling in disguise to Mecca, The Book of One Thousand Nights and A Night, an unexpurgated translation of One Thousand and One Nights, bringing the Kama Sutra to publication in English, and journeying with John Hanning Speke as the first Europeans led by Africa's greatest explorer guide, Sidi Mubarak Bombay, utilizing route information by Indian and Omani merchants who traded in the region, to visit the Great Lakes of Africa in search of the source of the Nile. Burton extensively criticized colonial policies (to the detriment of his career) in his works and letters. He was a prolific and erudite author and wrote numerous books and scholarly articles about subjects including human behavior, travel, fencing, sexual practices, and ethnography. A unique feature of his books is the copious footnotes and appendices containing remarkable observations and unexpurgated information. (Summary from Wikipedia)

Your monthly hit of the future of FM, cutting through all the noise (and the BS).

Dumas notes that "the details of this case are recorded in the archives of the Criminal Court at Naples." - Summary by jvanstan

description:
Propertymark Pulse, is a brand-new news bulletin for those who work within the property sector. 
Our aim is to deliver the very latest news, commentary, and expert opinion to keep professionals in touch with the latest happenings no matter if you are office based or out on the road.

title: the Garden Podcast
author: sofia obermaier
description: As a 22-year-old with no college degree but a full-time career in global missions, the Lord has taught me some things. The Garden is a space where I share those things with you! Join me every other Friday as I open a space for some life conversations. There is a whole lot of life to live and lessons to learn, and we wish we could do it all. Let’s navigate it together and learn much in the process.

title: Dark Ambient Sleep Stories - The Sleepless Atlas -

Dark sleep stories for the nights when your mind won't stop.

slowly dissolving your thoughts until you drift off.

title: Everyday Ayurveda with Kate
author: Kate O’Donnell
description:
Welcome to Everyday Ayurveda, a podcast by Kate O’Donnell, renowned Ayurvedic practitioner, bestselling author, and founder of the Ayurvedic Living Institute. Join Kate as she demystifies the ancient wisdom of Ayurveda and translates it into practical, everyday practices for modern living.
In each episode, Kate shares her deep knowledge and personal experiences from over two decades of studying Ayurveda in India. Whether you’re new to Ayurveda or a seasoned practitioner, you’ll discover valuable insights on diet, lifestyle, self-care, and holistic health.
Everyday Ayurveda is your go-to resource for integrating the timeless principles of Ayurveda into your daily routine, fostering a life of balance, health, and happiness. Subscribe now and start your journey towards radiant well-being with Kate O’Donnell.
Listen, learn, and transform with Everyday Ayurveda – because true health begins with the choices we make every day.

title: Brahma Sutra Shankara Bhashyam

description: The Brahma Sutra Shankara Bhashyam is one of the most profound and authoritative texts of Vedanta. Through Adi Shankaracharya’s commentary, the essential teachings of the Upanishads are presented with clarity, depth, and rigorous reasoning.Swami Tattwamayananda explains the Brahma Sutra Shankara Bhashyam word by word, guiding students through the profound teachings of Vedanta with clarity and depth.

title: Diary of a Community Builder
author: Rachel Starr
description: A daily dose of what building a community-led business actually looks like, from the founder of coCreator Society and host of the Community at Heart podcast. Every weekday morning at 6am, a new entry lands in your feed. A few minutes of what happened in the build the day before. The moments that made her pause, the aha moments of figuring out something new, the behind-the-scenes of running communities, memberships, and everything in between. No intro music. No polish. No highlight reel. Just the Tuesdays of building something real, because everyone shares the launch and nobody shares the Tuesday. Each entry ends with one journal prompt to carry into your own day of building. Listen for a few minutes, think for a few minutes, and start your morning a little more awake to what's happening in your own business. New entries drop Monday through Friday at 6am Eastern. Free to subscribe. The conversation happens inside coCreator Society.

Welcome to the S&K NETWORK Podcast! We’re a vibrant community dedicated to inspiring conversations that spark creativity, promote empowerment, and celebrate diversity. Our podcast features engaging interviews with thought leaders, innovators, and everyday heroes who share their insights and experiences. We dive into a variety of topics, from personal development and entrepreneurship to culture and technology, always with a fresh perspective. Whether you’re looking for motivation, practical tips, or just some good company during your commute, the S&K NETWORK Podcast has something for everyone. Welcome to spiriTED Talks with Donna Boyle
Donna Boyle—Medium, Direct Messenger for Spirit, and Author—invites you into these expansive spiriTED talks. title: The FYBS Playbook: Big Feelings, Real Caregiving — Toddlers to Teens
author: Feel Your Best Self
description:
Because big feelings don't come with instructions...
Whoever you are in a kid's life — parent, teacher, coach, the trusted adult down the hall — The FYBS Playbook meets you there. Hosted by psychologist Dr. Sandy Chafouleas and special educator Dr. Jess Koslouski, two moms who between them have clocked almost a century of raising kids, this podcast blends the research they've built careers on with the real-life caregiving moments that humbled them anyway. The Human Side of AI is a podcast for business owners and entrepreneurs who want to use AI in a way that actually fits their life and work. Each week, Mondays are led by our AI hosts diving into tools, trends, and practical applications, while Fridays bring real conversations with real humans using AI to work smarter and live fuller lives.
Because at the end of the day, we believe AI gives you something priceless back: time to be human.

title: NightWatch With Frank
author: NightWatch With Frank
description:
Every century has a night shift.
Frank has the watch. One night at a time, in a different year, Night Watch With Frank walks you through history after dark: the streets, the lamplight, and the people whose job it was to stay awake while everyone else slept. Nothing exciting happens in these episodes, on purpose. Put one on, follow the lantern, and drift off somewhere around the bridge. That's the job done well.
Every script is researched and written by a human. Frank is a Doberman in a flat cap. You'll get used to it.
New nights every week. The watch is over. Sleep well.

title: Lit or Quit

description:
Lit or Quit is for those readers out there who are exhausted by hype-machine mediocrity and Booktok groupthink. Whether tearing apart a bestseller everyone's supposed to love, or promoting a book that actually stole my heart, every verdict is based on the simple question: Is this book actually good?
I'll try to leave you each episode knowing exactly whether to read the book, understanding what made it work or fail, and maybe be entertained in the process.

title: Pulse PEMF Pulsecast
author: Pulse PEMF
description:
Transformative conversations with the doctors, athletes, biohackers, and innovators redefining health, recovery, and human performance. 

Unpacking the science, the stories, and the strategies behind Pulsed Electromagnetic Field (PEMF) therapy and whole-body wellness, from boosting cellular energy to accelerating recovery, optimizing sleep, and extending longevity. This is where next-level wellness begins.

‣ Upgrade your body
‣ Recharge your cells
‣ Recover faster

Hosted by the Pulse team, PulseCast pulls back the curtain on the technology trusted by pro teams across the NFL, MLB, NBA, NHL, and MLS. Celebrated as the gold standard of PEMF therapy and a proud sponsor of U.S. Figure Skating. 

Since 2006, Pulse has helped countless humans and animals naturally defy limits and come back stronger than before.

Whether you're an athlete, entrepreneur, practitioner, or simply looking to feel and function better, PulseCast delivers the actionable insights and inspiring conversations to help you take control of your well-being.

🎙️ New episodes every Thursday. Season 2 is out now! 

Tap in. Power up. Pulse on.

Learn more at pulsepemf.com

title: A Word for Life
author: D. Sheree Jacobs
description: A Word for Life is the Christ-centered teaching ministry of D. Sheree Jacobs dedicated to helping believers understand Scripture, grow in Christ, and apply biblical truth to everyday life. Through practical biblical teaching, prayer, and encouragement, each episode equips you to follow Christ faithfully and flourish in every season of life.

title: PWTRELYNEWS WEATHER
author: Elizabeth Caldwell
description: Weather surrounds us from outer space and on planet earth.

title: Art of Humanity
author: Jessica Ann
description:
The Art of Humanity with Jessica Ann explores the intersection of consciousness, technology, and modern entrepreneurship.
This is a podcast about how ideas shape reality, and how founders, creators, and thinkers become the architects of both.
Jessica moves beyond surface-level insights to uncover the deeper patterns driving business, identity, and influence in a rapidly shifting world.
This isn't just about creativity. 
It's about positioning.
Perception.
Power.
And the narratives that determine what gets built, and what gets believed.
Featuring conversations with voices like Seth Godin, Richard Rudd, Daniel Pinchbeck, Douglas Rushkoff, Danielle LaPorte, and more.
For founders and creators who understand:
the real work isn't just building companies
it's shaping the field they move in.

title: The Become Revolutionary Show
author: Noelle McGough & Charlene Helm
description:
Feeling alone in your business? Need inspiration to overcome challenges? Eager to discover the secrets of entrepreneurial resilience?


Join us as we pull back the curtain on insights from successful entrepreneurs, navigating the highs and lows of the journey.


WARNING! Our conversations will reshape your perspective – brace yourself for the ride!


Hosted by Charlene Helm & Noelle McGough, otherwise known as The Challenge Queens, The Become Revolutionary Show & Podcast is your compass. Take the leap today at BecomeRevolutionary.com to break free from business solitude.

title: Decoded Medicine
author: Decoded Medicine
description:
Close your eyes, imagine the human body at work, and let’s decode medicine together—one concept at a time.


By tapping into your auditory pathways and visual imagination, we will help encode medical concepts in your brain through understanding, not memorization.

title: Russian History Explained
author: Russian History Explained
description: Welcome to Russian History Explained, your ultimate guide to the vast and complex history of Russia. From the early medieval Kievan Rus and the rise of the powerful Tsars to the dramatic Soviet era and modern historical milestones, we explore the stories, figures, and events that shaped the world's largest nation. Through engaging narratives and rich archival visuals, we bring key historical turning points to life, exploring politics, culture, and epic conflicts. Join us as we uncover the mysteries of the Russian past.

title: BRoS Podcast
author: Michelle
description: Pull up a chair with BRoS. It's like a campfire conversation for the soul where real stories spark hope, courage, and connection. Each episode is an invitation to remember who you are and carry that light into your family, community, and the world.

title: Hazel’s Last Hand
author: Kate Colvin
description:
In a small Idaho logging town nestled among the pines, one murder changed everything.
More than thirty years later, the murder of Hazel Martin remains unsolved.
Hazel's Last Hand follows an active, independent investigation into what happened to Hazel in 1996. Through interviews with family members, friends, investigators, and those connected to the case, we revisit the events surrounding her disappearance and murder, reexamine the known facts, and explore the unanswered questions that continue to haunt this close-knit community. 
Because after 30 years, we haven’t forgotten.

title: Breaking News with Beatrice
author: Beatrice Njeri
description: AI Generated

title: State of the Union Addresses by United States Presidents (1923 - 1932)
author: Calvin Coolidge, Herbert Hoover
description: The State of the Union address is a speech presented by the President of the United States to a joint session of the United States Congress, typically delivered annually. The address not only reports on the condition of the nation but also allows the President to outline his legislative agenda (for which he needs the cooperation of Congress) and national priorities. This album contains recordings of addresses from Calvin Coolidge and Herbert Hoover. - Summary by Wikipedia

title: Fifty Years a Detective: 35 Real Detective Stories
author: Thomas Furlong
description: Hitherto unpublished facts connected with some of Mr. Furlong's greatest cases—Other interesting incidents of his long and strenuous career which really began on September 14, 1862, when he was detailed from his company, (Co. G., 1st Pennsylvania Rifles, better known as the Pennsylvania Bucktails) for special service. - Summary from the Preface

title: Dispatches from the Ruhr
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: Before finding celebrity as an author, including his 1954 Nobel Prize, Ernest M. Hemingway honed his craft as a journeyman reporter. In the spring of 1923, as a special correspondent for the Toronto Star, he travelled to the occupied Ruhr Valley where he produced a series of 10 articles, collected here as Dispatches from the Ruhr. In them, he explores the French political system and its role in the decision to occupy the Ruhr Valley militarily, in an effort to collect on unsustainable war reparations. In addition, he examines the suffering of its ordinary citizens, as conditions there led to a progressive loss of confidence in the Weimar Republic; its economic collapse under the weight of hyper-inflation; and, ultimately, to the rise of Nazism. It is worth reading as both a case study on the unintended consequences of military occupation and a master class in the development of Hemingway’s characteristic prose style. - Summary by ASharma

title: Yorkshire Battles
author: Edward Lamplough
description: Edward Lamplough describes 22 battles that all occurred in Yorkshire over many time periods and political contexts. - Summary by lightcrystal

title: Literature of Arabia
author: Epiphanius Wilson
description: A collection of many romantic stories: it has no epic unity. It will remind the reader of the "Morte d'Arthur" of Sir Thomas Malory, rather than of the "Iliad." The author chose the most striking of these episodes as best calculated to serve as genuine specimens of Arabian literature. They will transport the modern reader into a new world—which is yet the old, long vanished world of pastoral simplicity and warlike enthusiasm, in primitive Arabia. - Summary by Epiphanius Wilson

title: Renaissance and the Reformation: A Textbook of European History 1494-1610
author: Emmeline Tanner
description: Dame Emmeline Tanner writes of the Renaissance that its "special characteristic was the revolt against authority and the rise to importance of the individual." Politically, "the renaissance marks the death of the idea of the universal authority of the Empire." Ecclesiastically, "the period marks the breaking-up of the idea of the World Church and the rise of national churches. The authority of the Church was overthrown, and in Teutonic lands the Renaissance became the Reformation." This is a book about broad concepts and remarkable individuals: the Borgia Pope, Alexander VI, the Emperor Charles V, Queen Elizabeth of England, Martin Luther, John Calvin, William the Silent, and Philip II of Spain and his terrible Inquisition. (Summary by Pamela Nagami, M.D.)

title: Backwash Of War: The Human Wreckage Of The Battlefield As Witnessed By An American Hospital Nurse
author: Ellen Newbold La Motte
description: Ellen Newbold La Motte (1873–1961) was an American nurse, journalist and author. … and in 1915 volunteered as one of the first American war nurses to go to Europe and treat soldiers in World War I. In Belgium she served in a French field hospital, keeping a bitter diary detailing the horrors that she witnessed daily. “I am a professor of American studies and recently spent several years researching the life of Ellen N. La Motte, a long-forgotten nurse and public health crusader. In particular, I focused on her war writing. Soon after World War I began, she volunteered as a nurse in a French field hospital; later she published an explosive book of stories, “The Backwash of War,” about the experience. I spent endless hours immersed in those deeply unsettling and darkly humorous tales of wounded and sick hospitalized soldiers…. Cynthia Wachtell is a research associate professor of American studies at Yeshiva University…” (New York Times 22 May 2020)

title: Boots and Saddles
author: Elizabeth Bacon Custer
description: Elizabeth Custer has penned an engaging portrait of 1870’s life on a U.S. cavalry post in the Dakotas, just before her husband and his troops met their tragic deaths in the Battle of the Little Big Horn. “Our life,” she writes, “was often as separate from the rest of the world as if we had been living on an island in the ocean.” Her portrait of her husband, General George Armstrong Custer is laudatory—his intellect, his love of dogs (he kept a hunting pack of 40 at the post); but, Boots and Saddles is more than just a memorial. She observes with keen insight, the varied persons, from Indian scouts, to enlisted men, to officer’s wives, who make up the army “family,” on the post. Her sympathetic story about the regimental laundress and midwife, with its sad ending, should take a place in the army’s history of “don’t ask, don’t tell.” (Summary by Sue Anderson)

title: The Cyber-Savvy Boardroom
author: David Homovich
description:
Strategic Clarity for the Modern Executive: Produced by Google Cloud's Office of the CISO, this monthly podcast cuts through the noise of the technical landscape to deliver actionable insights directly to the boardroom table. In about 20 minutes, we bridge the gap between complex cyber threats and strategic organizational resilience.
Your host is David Homovich, alongside Alicja Cade and Nick Godfrey. Each episode guest is an expert with titles like Board members, Google Cloud (and Google) execs, Office of the CISO members of the Board Insights team, board advisors, and industry thought leaders.

title: Liberation: Marines in the Recapture of Guam
author: Cyril J. O'Brien
description: Guam became a territorial possession of the United States with the signing of the Treaty of Paris in 1899, ending the Spanish-American War. Earlier, on 21 June 1898, First Lieutenant John Twiggs “Handsome Jack” Myers had led a party of Marines ashore from the protected cruiser Charleston to accept the surrender of the Spanish authorities. Thus began a long Marine presence on Guam. The island, southernmost of the Marianas chain, was discovered by Ferdinand Magellan in 1521, but not occupied until 1688 when a small mission was established there by a Spanish priest and soldiers. When control of the rest of the Mariana Islands was given to Japan as a mandate power in 1919, Guam became an isolated and highly vulnerable American outpost in a Japanese sea. This American territory, 35 miles long, nine miles at its widest and four at its narrowest, shaped like a peanut fell quickly and easily in the early morning of 10 December 1941. With the opening of a two-hour, ever-increasing bombardment by six battleships, nine cruisers, a host of destroyers and rocket ships, laying their wrath on the wrinkled black hills, rice paddies, cliffs, and caves that faced the attacking fleet on the west side of the island, liberation day for Guam began at 0530, 21 July 1944. - Summary by Cyril J. O’Brien

title: British Soldier: His Courage And Humour
author: Edward John Hardy
description: The Reverend Edward John Hardy, ordained in the Church Of Ireland, was a chaplain in the British army for thirty one years. His 1915 collection of anecdotes is by and about the British soldier, the “Tommy” or “Mr. Thomas Atkins” as Hardy calls him. “I did not need a war of nations to learn about the courage and humour of the British soldier…. . I have done little more than select and classify the letters of that best of war correspondents—the British soldier. The letters are a credit to his head and his heart, and throw a searchlight on the war. The soldier wrote of the things he knew about, and the result is that we can see his pen pictures….” - Summary by Book Preface and david wales

title: Romance Of Modern Sieges
author: Edward Gilliat
description: Lively narratives of some of the great siege battles of war. The book was written before World War I, in 1908. Some of the narratives contain language which was common in 19th and early twentieth century usage but which listeners today may find offensive. Summary by david wales

title: Fighting France, from Dunkerque to Belfort
author: Edith Wharton
description: American novelist Edith Wharton was living in Paris when World War I broke out in 1914. She obtained permission to visit sites behind the lines, including hospitals, ravaged villages, and trenches. Fighting France records her travels along the front in 1914 and 1915, and celebrates the indomitable spirit of the French people. (Summary by Elizabeth Klett)

title: Fighting the Flying Circus
author: Eddie Rickenbacker
description: This is the WWI memoirs of Medal of Honor winner, Capt Eddie Rickenbacker. He fought in and eventually became commander of the 94th "Hat-in-the-Ring" Squadron, which ended the war with the highest number of air victories of any American squadron. The circus mentioned in the title refers to the German squadron commanded by the famous Red Baron, Manfred von Richthofen. (Introduction by Brett W. Downey)

title: The Psychology of Power
author: SHERO CYBER
description: The Psychology of Power is a groundbreaking executive podcast series that exposes the hidden psychological patterns that turn leaders into organizational risks. Through forensic analysis, real‑world case patterns, and research from organizational psychology, governance, and enterprise risk, this series reveals how ego, insecurity, favoritism, and retaliation quietly destabilize culture, derail strategy, and erode enterprise value. Designed for boards, CEOs, CHROs, governance committees, organizational psychologists, and leadership scholars, this series reframes leadership failure not as a performance issue — but as a psychological system with measurable impact on: governance integrity financial stewardship cultural health operational maturity legal exposure long‑term business sustainability Each episode blends psychology, governance science, and real organizational patterns to help leaders recognize the early warning signs of ego‑driven leadership — and build cultures where maturity, ethics, and accountability thrive. This is not just a leadership podcast. This is a governance intervention.

title: Notes of a Camp Follower on the Western Front
author: E. W. Hornung
description: In 1915 Oscar Hornung, son of the famous author E W Hornung, was killed at Ypres after less than a year as a soldier in Flanders. He was only 20. Two years later E W Hornung volunteered to help run one of the YMCA canteens close behind the front line. This book is Hornung’s own account of the time he spent in Northern France: first helping in a canteen, then running a library for the enlisted men. He wanted to be near the place where his son died, to meet the young soldiers who were fighting the war, and to make their lives a little better. More than anything, Hornung wanted to believe there was a greater purpose to he war: in his descriptions soldiers are always heroes, the struggles just, and leaders wise and kind. But whatever his motivations and blind-spots, Hornung brought all his skills as a highly experienced novelist to the task of telling his story. We feel we are there with him while he talks to the soldiers, travels to the front line to serve cocoa and biscuits under fire, and finally sets up a lending library only a couple of miles from No Man’s Land. Even Sir Arthur Conan Doyle (a former friend who reported Hornung to the authorities for promoting pacifism) grudgingly admitted that this book contains some of the best descriptions of life on the Western Front. (The cover shows the western front as it passed trough the town of Arras in 1918 at the time the author would have known it) (Summary by Clive Catterall)

title: British Army From Within
author: E. Charles Vivian
description: This 1914 book gives a picture of the British Army structure and life in the early hours of World War I. Summary by david wales

title: Martyrdom of Man
author: (William) Winwood Reade
description: William Winwood Reade (1838 - 1875) was a British historian, explorer, and philosopher.
