Things Stanford Never Taught me

Career Building and Life long learning 

Becoming a STEF leader

AI reinvention: Human x AI 

Preserving Health 

from Income to impact 

Leave a Legacy

title: That's A Good Word Bro
author: Adam Ybarra
description: Reading and commenting from the Book of Ezekiel. Each podcast looks at the undertone of verses to fuel faith and highlights on practical application.

title: The Recognize Podcast
author: Zach Woodke
description: The Recognize Podcast is built on one unshakable truth: you already have what it takes. The dream on your heart isn’t random. It's yours for a reason. You weren’t given vision by accident, and you weren’t wired to live small. Greatness isn’t reserved for a few - it’s built by those who choose to see it in themselves. This podcast is about clarity, ownership, faith, growth and the courage to step fully into who you’re meant to become. This is for those who think deeply, question often and refuse to live unconsciously.

title: Safety And Risk Success
author: Christian Harris
description:
Bring the benefits of proactive safety and risk management to life, through insights, experiences and stories.
Every week, host Christian Harris - the UK's leading slips, trips and falls specialist - presents a new episode, typically with expert guests, to discuss:
- Safety
- Health
- Wellbeing
- Insurance
- Risk management
- Claims defensibility

title: Any Connector Podcast
author: Any Connector
description: Unlock the power of seamless integrations with Any Connector. In this show, we explore how businesses can connect their favorite apps, tools, and platforms to streamline workflows, boost productivity, and save valuable time. Whether you’re a business owner, IT professional, or tech enthusiast, you’ll discover practical insights, use cases, and best practices for making integrations work smarter for you.

title: פוצ׳י
author: בית הפודיום
description: פודקאסט הפוצ׳יוולי של בית הפודיום, עם רון בן ישי, אורי צידון ותומר ראב

title: Homecoming Podcast
author: Chae Pounds and Isis Rose
description:
At Homecoming, we address two myths: 1) Hospitals are the safest place to give birth and 2) Black families don't birth at home. We broadcast how Black families birth in love and choose to birth at home, unassisted or with midwives. We interview parents, birth workers, cloth diaper enthusiasts and also feature stories of hospital transfer during planned home births and even stories of loss. Join us as we discover Black homebirth stories in the context of the national crisis in maternal and infant health. In spite of the statistics, we DO have beautiful births! 

Welcome home.

title: Underknown Network
author: EB, Jae Mac, CB DaBully, J Milly,
description: Home to two of the podcasts from the Underknown Network; On the Map & Recognize Game

title: Lauren Lately
author: Lauren Helen
description: a casual space for life updates, creative side quests, wellness, film industry stories, & the occasional unnecessary opinion 🫶🏼

title: Know Your Rights Camp Podcast Presents: Camp Convos
author: Know Your Rights Camp Podcast
description: Know Your Rights Camp Podcast Presents: Camp Convos goes behind the scenes with the presenters and presentations that power the Know Your Rights Camps. Each episode pulls back the curtain on what it takes to educate and empower the next generation of leaders in Black and Brown communities.

title: The LinguaFox Investigation Series
author: Abbas Salahshour
description:
LinguaFox Investigation Series is Season 2 of LinguaFox: Your Shortcut to Fluent Speech.
This documentary-style podcast investigates the biggest myths, questions, and assumptions in language learning. Instead of quick tips, each episode opens a Case File exploring evidence from linguistics, psychology, education, and real learning experience. We separate belief from reality and rethink how fluency actually develops. Open the next Case File.

title: Valuestream
author: Rick Pollick
description: A podcast from Rick Pollick on how modern companies actually turn strategy into shipped software. Every episode walks one real story through three segments — intake, flow, outcome — covering operating models, platform engineering, and agentic-AI patterns that move value from the roadmap to production. 25 to 35 minutes, monthly. No vendor pitches. Focus is the operating model, not the tool stack. Companion essays at rickpollick.com/blog.

title: END OF THE AGE PODCAST
author: WITNESS TO ALL THE NATIONS
description:
We’re living in a pivotal moment—where ancient prophecies and current events converge. Did you know that around 27% of the Bible is predictive prophecy?
END OF THE AGE PODCAST dives deep into biblical eschatology, the rise of antisemitism, and the global influence of Islam. Grounded firmly in Scripture, we encourage listeners to be like the Bereans—examining everything in the light of God’s Word. Join us as we unpack the prophetic timeline and equip you to understand the times and stand firm in truth.

title: What if it F*cking Works Out?!
author: Leslie McGregor
description:
What If It Fucking Works Out?! is a podcast for anyone who’s ready to stop obsessing over everything that could go wrong. Join me each week as I explore mindset shifts, manifestation, psychology, energy, and the occasional random shower thought...all with one goal: helping you spend less time expecting the worst and more time believing in what’s possible.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: CHUCHO EL ROTO- la radionovela
author: systemfail
description:
Hubo un tiempo en que las familias se reunían alrededor de un radio para dejar que la imaginación hiciera el resto. No había pantallas, no había botones para adelantar ni plataformas donde ver el capítulo después. Solo una voz, una historia y el suspenso de esperar al día siguiente.Hoy queremos revivir esa experiencia con el reestreno de la radionovela original de Chucho el Roto, respetando su esencia, sus voces, su música y la forma en que conquistó a generaciones enteras.A partir de esta semana estrenaremos un capítulo cada semana, tal como se vivía en aquella época. Y como entonces, si te lo pierdes... tendrás que esperar un buen tiempo para volver a escucharlo. La idea es volver a disfrutar la emoción de la espera, comentar la historia con la familia y dejar que cada episodio permanezca en la memoria.Prepara el radio, los audífonos o la bocina de tu preferencia. La leyenda de Chucho el Roto está de regreso, exactamente como miles de familias la escucharon por primera vez.
La historia mezclaba hechos históricos con mucha ficción. Su protagonista era Jesús Arriaga Zamora, un humilde carpintero que termina convertido en un ladrón caballeroso después de ser víctima de las injusticias del Porfiriato. Es, básicamente, el Robin Hood mexicano:
  • Robaba a los ricos.
  • Ayudaba a los pobres.
  • Burlaba constantemente a la policía.
  • Escapaba una y otra vez de la prisión de San Juan de Ulúa.
  • Siempre conservaba modales de caballero.
Lo que hacía especial a la radionovela era su producción. No tenía explosiones hechas por computadora ni efectos digitales. Todo era imaginación:
  • puertas que rechinaban,
  • caballos galopando,
  • espadas,
  • cadenas,
  • lluvia,
  • disparos,
  • música orquestal.


Nos escuchamos en el próximo capítulo. No faltes.

title: Grow Where You're Planted
author: Heather Price
description: Grow Where You’re Planted is a podcast hosted by executive coach and leadership strategist Heather Price. Rooted in practical wisdom and powerful insights, each episode is designed to help you maximize your influence, lead with clarity, and grow—right where you are. Whether you're navigating change, leading a team, or leveling up in life, Heather brings coaching conversations, real-world strategies, and inspiring stories to help you thrive in your current season. Ready to grow? Let’s get started.

title: The Gunpowder Coven
author: Andi Jo, Jim and The Gunpowder Witch
description: Join Andi, Jim and Moonpie, as they discuss topics centered around the inclusive 2a community.

title: Activity Coaching Conversations
author: Heather Price and Sabine Robinson
description:
Hey everyone, welcome to activity coaching conversations with Heather and Sabine, where we discuss coaching strategies for the overachievers, the slow starters and everyone in between. We're going to share everything we know about instilling early success habits, the science behind activity, and how to build a values-based FR centered coaching philosophy. Activity Coaching Conversations is all about balancing accountability with the art and science of coaching.
Each episode will highlight best practices and useful language featured in our “Say That Again”, segment and real-world application because let's face it, theories don't always work in the real world. We're dealing with humans after all. With that in mind, we want you to feel re-energized about coaching with a fresh perspective and practical strategies that you can use immediately. 
Hosted by your activity coaching gurus, Sabine Robinson, and Heather Price. Stay in the loop on all things activity coaching by checking out the links below in the show notes. You can also support the show by listening, liking and sharing in your favorite podcast app. So, buckle up and join us on this new adventure.

title: Mind Up
author: Glen Kochaney
description: Mind Up is a podcast delivering intellectual content of Health and Lifestyle.

title: The Challenge Coin Podcast
author: Strike Your Coin
description:
Every challenge coin tells a story. Behind every coin is a person, a mission, and a moment worth remembering.
The Challenge Coin Podcast features the real stories behind custom challenge coins - the veterans, first responders, leaders, and teams who commissioned them and why. Hosted by Artur Ishkaev, founder of Strike Your Coin, Canada's custom challenge coin company.
If you've ever held a coin and wondered about the story behind it - this show is for you.

title: The Vaughn Kohler Podcast
author: Vaughn Kohler
description:
Most people I meet are carrying more than they let on.
They're founders, parents, pastors, executives, and leaders. They're successful in many of the ways that matter, yet beneath the surface they often feel lost. Not in a dramatic sense, but in the ordinary modern sense. Pulled in too many directions. Disconnected from what matters most. Moving quickly without always knowing where they're headed.
I know the feeling.
Each week, I share a brief reflection on faith, wisdom, relationships, meaningful work, and the lifelong process of becoming fully human. Drawing on lessons from ministry, leadership, books, suffering, beauty, and everyday life, I explore the questions that shape a meaningful life: Who am I? What do I want? What am I afraid of? What's next?
The subject changes. The mission doesn't.
Everything here is an attempt to help us become less scattered, less lonely, more rooted, and more fully alive.
If you're trying to strengthen your faith, deepen your relationships, lead with wisdom, or simply find your footing in a noisy world, you're welcome here.
Let's find our way together.
Subscribe to My Substack: https://vaughnkohler.substack.com/
Follow me on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/vaughnkohler
Visit my website: https://www.vaughnkohler.com/

title: The Poppy Day Bomb
author: The Times
description:
A bombing kills 11 people attending the Remembrance Sunday commemorations in the small town of Enniskillen, Northern Ireland. A twelfth victim would die in a coma 13 years later.


No one has ever been charged.


A lifetime later, The Times’s reporter Mario Ledwith returns to his hometown, asking who carried out these bloody murders, how the authorities failed to fully investigate, and why a silence continues to surround what happened. 

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Personal Finance Project
author: The Personal Finance Project
description:
The Personal Finance Project is a podcast dedicated to providing specialized advice on retirement planning, investment strategies, and financial management, particularly for employees in the oil, gas, and chemical industries. Hosted by experienced advisors from Baird Retirement Management, each episode delves into topics designed to help listeners make informed decisions about their financial futures. Whether you're approaching retirement or looking to optimize your investment portfolio, this show offers valuable insights tailored to your unique situation. Tune in to stay informed and take control of your financial well-being.
Meet Your Hosts:
•Greg Ashcroft, CFA®, CFP®, CPWA®, CIMA®, RMA®:
Greg is a Senior Vice President at Baird Retirement Management. He joined Baird in May 2015 after graduating from Texas A&M University with a Bachelor of Business Administration in Management. Greg has earned multiple professional designations, including Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA®), CERTIFIED FINANCIAL PLANNER™ (CFP®), Certified Private Wealth Advisor® (CPWA®), Certified Investment Management Analyst® (CIMA®), and Retirement Management Advisor® (RMA®). In March 2023, he graduated with a Master of Science in Personal Financial Planning.
•Scott Bartosh, CFP®:
Scott is a Director at Baird Retirement Management and holds the CERTIFIED FINANCIAL PLANNER™ certification, demonstrating his commitment to providing comprehensive financial planning services. Scott also holds a Master of Science in Personal Financial Planning, highlighting his dedication to advancing his expertise in helping clients navigate their financial futures.
Together, Greg and Scott bring a wealth of knowledge and experience to the podcast, offering listeners practical advice and strategies to navigate the complexities of retirement planning and investment management.

title: The Do Gooders Podcast
author: Christin Thieme
description: If you care about making an impact for good then the Do Gooders Podcast from The Salvation Army's Caring Magazine is the show for you. Hosted by Editor-in-Chief Christin Thieme, listen in for real-life inspiration, expert insight, evidence of transformation happening in communities across the U.S. through The Salvation Army, and ideas from change-makers so you can join in right where you are and be the reason somebody believes in goodness.

title: Curious for Fun
author: Sasha DeMarre
description:
Welcome to Curious for Fun, a walk-and-talk podcast for learners, experimenters, and curious humans who love exploring what makes life interesting. 
I share what I’m learning, trying, and noticing - from creative experiments to reflections on growth, psychology, and connection. Along the way, I pull ideas from books, conversations, and everyday moments that spark curiosity. All in the spirit of joy, learning, and being human.

title: The DefencIQ Podcast
author: Andrew Egoroff
description:
The DefencIQ Podcast

Practical, plain-English guidance for Australian SMEs navigating Defence Industry Security Program (DISP) membership, Essential Eight compliance, and the security requirements of working in the Australian Defence supply chain.

Hosted by Andrew Egoroff, co-founder of DefencIQ, each episode breaks down a single topic — DISP levels and domains, Essential Eight Maturity Level 2, security clearances, gap assessments, and the mistakes SMEs commonly make — without the jargon or the sales pitch.

Whether a Defence prime has told you that you need DISP, or you're planning ahead before it becomes a contract requirement, this podcast gives you the straight answer in the time it takes to have a coffee.

New episodes drawn from real questions we hear from Australian defence subcontractors, engineering firms, and professional services businesses entering or deepening their involvement in the Defence sector.

Learn more or book a free DISP Quick Start Assessment at defenciq.com.au

title: The AI Compass
author: Reuters Plus
description:
The AI Compass is a podcast series for companies looking to move beyond surface level AI adoption toward enterprise-wide integration. Host Chloe Tilley interviews executives from around the world focused on maintaining momentum amid today's AI transformation.

Produced by Reuters Plus for KPMG

Visit the Thomson Reuters Privacy Statement for information on our privacy and data protection practices.  

You may also visit megaphone.fm/adchoices to opt-out of targeted advertising.

title: Marketing Beyond with Alan B. Hart
author: Alan B. Hart
description: Join Alan B. Hart as he dives into conversations with the world's leading chief marketing officers and business innovators. Tune in to get inspired by the stories and experiences of marketing visionaries—and stay ahead of the curve.

title: Net Assessment
author: The Stimson Center
description: Hosts Melanie Marlowe, Christopher Preble, and Zack Cooper, debate their way through some of the toughest and most contentious topics related to strategy, international relations, and the use of force. This podcast is brought to you by the Stimson Center and produced by University FM.

title: RNN Cast
author: Radio Network News
description: Your essential daily news brief. This feed delivers crisp, accurate, and completely up-to-the-minute audio bulletins directly to your device. We skip the opinion and focus entirely on the facts, giving you a clear snapshot of the headlines as they break.

title: Marketing Team of One
author: Page Design Group
description: Struggling as a solo marketer? Tune into our podcast for strategic insights, inspiring stories, and a dash of humor to empower you as a Marketing Team of One. With Eric and Mike's extensive experience in design, marketing, and running a successful agency, they are here to help transform your marketing challenges into victories.

title: Spontaneous Side Stories
author: Jonah S
description: Spontaneous Side Stories is a cozy, narrative‑driven podcast where personal memories, pop‑culture obsessions, and unexpected tangents collide. Hosted by Jonah, each episode blends storytelling, reflection, and a dash of playful chaos!

title: RetroActive Podcast
author: Fred Rojas
description: A podcast examining the culture of retro video games. Each episode provides an in depth analysis on a specific title, developer, series, or topic and examines the influences on the history of gaming. As a variety show, additional mini sections are dedicated to topics like hardware, modifications, or other interesting factors of contemporary retro gaming. Hosted by Fred Rojas of gaminghistory101.com and featuring guest co-hosts, it's a new look at an old topic.

title: Pretty Strange
author: Taylor Madison
description: Pretty Strange is a podcast about history, mystery, and all the weird things nobody taught you in school. From lost cities and conspiracy theories to scandals, legends, and bizarre true stories, I'm diving into the strange side of reality—because the truth is usually weirder than fiction.

title: Daily Funs.
author: Daily Funs.
description: Daily Funs delivers a refreshing blend of comedy, storytelling, and entertainment designed to add more joy to your day. Each episode brings engaging conversations, amusing discoveries, and memorable moments that celebrate the funny side of life.Press play and join a community that believes every day deserves a little more laughter.

title: BRAVE Church
author: Jeff Schwarzentraub
description: Dig deep into the Bible.  Explore the truths of God's word. Apply those concepts to your life and grow deeper in who God made you.  Listen to the podcasts from BRAVE.

title: Fairy Tales for Alice
author: Storyteller Jan
description:
Meet Ollie, a small, fluffy black puppy with one ear up and one ear down, and a very big secret: his doghouse is a time machine.
Every episode, Ollie travels somewhere new, from ancient Egypt to a medieval castle to the Ice Age, meeting new friends and learning real history along the way.
Cozy audio stories for children under 8, perfect for bedtime, car rides, or curling up on the couch.
✨ Wonder and gentle surprises
📚 Real history, real facts
💛 A meaningful lesson every time
🎧 Made for little ears and big imaginations
No screens needed. Full Cast.

title: Island Legends
author: Sam Wickstrand
description: Join young Orcas Island host Sam Wickstrand on his quest to uncover the island’s soul with Island Legends! This short-form podcast features chats with legendary locals - nature lovers, artists, musicians, writers, mariners, chefs, farmers, business owners, old-timers, eccentrics, free spirits, and more. Discover what makes Orcas Island so cool! Listen on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, YouTube, and soon on KIXP 102.3 FM and KIXP.org. Celebrate the legends who call Orcas home!

title: Mystic Mates
author: Biscuit Jim
description:
We pulled a Tarot... and here we are. Helen George & Yvette Robinson are your new Mystic Mates - here for best stories of readings (good and bad), crystal energy, astrological life choices and more. This is the place to enjoy your mystic side!
New episodes every Monday - follow to ensure you never miss an episode.

title: NewSingle
author: NewSingle
description: dopekond's upcoming single blends dark R&B with raw emotion, telling a story of envy, fake people, heartbreak, and the determination to rise above negativity. Mixing Moroccan Darija with English, the track reflects personal struggles, emotional isolation, and the decision to leave the past behind while chasing a future through music.

title: I'd Watch That
author: BIG Words Media
description:
The film industry had its chance.
Now Geoff and Rick are fixing it.
Welcome to “I’d Watch That!”
Guided by the audience and a random roll of the dice, Rick and Geoff create movie magic in real time. All you have to do is listen, enjoy, and ask yourself if I'd Watch That?

title: The Lost Signal by Lost and Loud Creative Studio
author: Lost and Loud Creative Studio
description: The Lost Signal is a home for deeply-researched, plainly-told science mini-series about the hidden workings of the living world.

title: Built to Last
author: Hearst StoryStudio
description: This podcast will teach you why from cooling to heating, or plumbing repairs, Comfort-Air Engineering and Primo Plumbing are the best when it comes to delivering quality service and expert workmanship.

title: National Weather Service SOUTH MIAMI DADE COUNTY FLORIDA FORECAST - by WARN
author: Weather Alert Radio Network
description:
National Weather Service forecast for South Dade County Florida including the Everglades
FLZ174
https://forecast.weather.gov/MapClick.php?zoneid=FLZ174

title: The Wisdom Seat Podcast
author: The Wisdom Seat
description:
The Wisdom Seat is a group of Buddhist teachers and practitioners residing in the Mid-Atlantic region who host spiritual teachers, sponsor meditation retreats and offer support to those on the Buddhist path. 
Visit us at thewisdomseat.org

Drawing on the profound view of the Kagyu-Nyingma and Rimé traditions of Tibetan Buddhism, The Wisdom Seat seeks to bring these spiritual traditions into our modern-day life – cultivating healthy relationships between teachers and students and reliably transmitting the Buddhist teachings to others.

title: CTK: THE SERMON SERIES
author: Christ The King MKE
description: Never miss a message from CTK! Whether you couldn’t make it on Sunday or just want to listen again, the CTK Sermons podcast brings every inspiring sermon right to your ears. Tune in anytime for uplifting words, fresh perspectives, and encouragement for your week—wherever you are. Everyone’s welcome, every sermon, every Sunday!

title: The East Africa Travel Podcast by Diary of a Muzungu
author: Charlotte Beauvoisin
description:
The *award-winning East Africa Travel Podcast is hosted by Charlotte Beauvoisin, author of the popular travel blog Diary of a Muzungu. The podcast is inspired by Charlotte's life in a wooden house at Sunbird Hill on the edge of Kibale Forest in western Uganda, a National Park that is famous for chimpanzees, forest elephants, African grey parrots and Africa's biggest butterfly!
Tune in for:
- The dawn chorus of a tropical rainforest, gorilla tracking adventures and the sound of waves lapping on an Indian Ocean beach.
- Morning nature walks (gum boots on!)
- Featured destinations from across Uganda, Kenya, Tanzania and Rwanda
- Personal, well-researched travel advice
- Illuminating chats with nature lovers, trail-blazing conservationists and intrepid travellers
- Stories, anecdotes (and strictly positive vibes)
- Winner of African Travel and Tourism Association ATTA® Media Voice for Action Award 2025 
- Season 2 - another 20 weekly episodes - is now live. Join us!

title: The Loneliest Road
author: Beaux
description:
On the loneliest road in America, truck driver Beaux records the strange stories of the hitchhikers he picks up. 
When his tapes start capturing impossible sounds, Beaux and his wife are pulled into a mystery buried deep in the Nevada desert. A slow-burn supernatural audio drama about isolation, trust, and the voices that call from the dark.

title: They Play Games
author: They Play Games
description:
They Play Games is a podcast aimed at Gender Non Conforming Bodies within the gaming world, be it gamers or the people behind the games.  
It was created so folks were able to share their stories and so others can also hear from them.

title: King Earthquake Frequencies
author: Errol Arawak
description:
King Earthquake Podcast – The Sound System Chronicles 🎶🔥
Welcome to the King Earthquake Podcast, the home of heavyweight bass, roots & culture, and pure sound system power!
Join us as we go back in time and relive legendary sessions, deep dive into the history of reggae & dub, and explore the Word, Sound, and Power that has shaped the foundation of the culture.
Expect exclusive interviews, rare recordings, sound system stories, and deep selections straight from the King Earthquake vaults. Whether you're a longtime reggae warrior or new to the scene, this podcast delivers the real sound system experience—anytime, anywhere.
🎧 Turn up the bass & feel the vibrations!
#KingEarthquakePodcast #SoundSystemCulture #HeavyweightBass #ReggaeVibes #Dubwise #RootsAndCulture

title: Flirty Fusion
author: Mr and Ms Fusion
description:
Flirty Fusion is an honest fun and judgement free pod cast hosted by Mr & Ms Fusion 
We dive into the world of ethical non-monogamy and the swinging lifestyle, sharing our own experiences, the highs, the challenges, and everything we’ve learned along the way From first time nerves and unforgettable adventures to building trust, communication, and genuine connections, no topic is off Whether you’re simply curious, new to the lifestyle, experienced, or just love hearing authentic conversations about relationships, intimacy, and personal growth come along for the journey.
Welcome to Flirty Fusion

title: Significant Women
author: Kinexis
description:
Significant Women is a podcast about family, wealth and what it takes to hold both together across generations.
Every episode, Georgia Barkell sits down with a woman from a significant family. Entrepreneurs, matriarchs, inheritors, the next generation. They talk about the things that rarely get said out loud: the values, the pressure, the responsibility and what legacy actually means when real money and real family are involved.
Subscribe for new stories every month.
Georgia Barkell is the founder of Kinexis, an independent advisory firm working with significant families. www.kinexis.au

title: Minding Your Bizness Podcast
author: Minding Your Bizness Podcast
description: 🎙 Minding Your Bizness Podcast is your no-fluff guide to building a stronger business brain. Hosted by Cher-Ron Thicklin, we dive into mindset, branding, contracts, partnerships, marketing, and growth strategies to help you level up. For entrepreneurs who’ve failed, fought, and still refuse to quit—this is your space to learn, laugh, and take action. 🌐 blackwallstreetnation.com | 📅 bit.ly/meetwithcherron

title: The Sydney Table
author: The Sydney Table
description:
Sydney has no shortage of opinions and opinionated leaders. What it lacks is honest, informed and respectful conversation between people who have the power to change things.
The Sydney Table brings the ideas, debates and provocations to you. Hosted by Christopher Brown AM, each episode takes on the news and issues our city can't afford to ignore and hosts the conversations it needs to have. 
See you at The Table.

title: Living An Extraordinary Life  with Pastor Mikaela Cade
author: Mikaela Cade
description:
Life can be challenging, especially when trying to live by faith. How do you live with confidence when you have faith and the mountain doesn‘t move? How do you know what to do when you‘re wondering if God really answers prayer? How do you live with joy and peace when there is a big difference between what ”they” told you and your everyday experience? The questions seem endless. 
Each week, join Pastor Mikaela Cade, author and life strategist devoted to helping believers deepen their faith, understand their purpose, and live with passion as she discusses how to take the guesswork out of faith-filled living. Real talk about helping you bridge the gap between faith and experience. Whether you are a long-time believer, just starting your journey, or looking to strengthen your faith, this podcast is for you. #yourggrowuppastor

title: Where are you now?
author: School of Pharmacy and Medical Sciences
description: Where are you now? is the podcast that follows real journeys of graduates from health, pharmacy and medical science into the careers they never expected, and the ones they always dreamed of. Each episode dives into the twists and defining moments that shaped our guests' careers. From unexpected detours and career changing decisions, to lessons learned and words of wisdom, you'll hear honest conversations that go beyond the resume. No matter where you are in your life journey, this podcast will inspire you. Because the path isn't always straight, but it can take you somewhere extraordinary.

title: The NOWCAST
author: NOWISM
description:
The NOWCAST is a daily conversation for recovering escapists seeking clarity, presence & intentional living.
Hosted by ANDY—a human first, part monk, part artist—exploring attention, awareness, emotional regulation, modern life & the timeless practice of returning to the now.
Together, we explore the small escapes of everyday life, uncover the deeper signal beneath the noise, and find north again through reflection, practical wisdom & guided moments of stillness.
If you've ever felt distracted, overwhelmed, or disconnected from yourself, this is your daily return.
Exit Escapism. Enter Now.

title: Reverence for Impulse
author: Weena Pauly-Tarr and Tim Pauly
description: An improvised podcast about people and their impulses.

title: Respect The Process
author: Jordan Brady
description:
One-on-one chats with commercial directors, advertising agency creatives, actors, editors, producers, crew and more. Filmmaker Jordan Brady has directed of over 1300 spots , 4 feature films, 3 full-length documentaries and one Netflix special. The show is edited by Jake Brady.
Jordan is Founder of Commercial Directing Film School and owner of True Gentleman Industries, aka True Gent, a commercial production company in Los Angeles. Respect The Process, since 2013.

title: Let's Name It
author: Rachel Baptiste
description:
Let’s Name It is a space for conversations on being human, leadership, belonging, and what often goes unnamed beneath the surface of our workplaces, relationships, and systems.
Hosted by Rachel Baptiste, Human-First Leadership strategist, Coach, and Founder of Lumen Consulting Group Inc, this show explores the hidden patterns, relational truths, and lived experiences that shape how we lead, work, and belong. These are reflective conversations for and with leaders, practitioners, and humans willing to look more honestly at what shapes lived experience and what we have learned to leave unnamed.

title: Unshakeable People
author: Katie Kaspari
description: The world is shaking, changing with a power that makes the most basic things uncertain. Every day something valuable far beyond money and stuff gets taken away from us — it takes away our BALANCE. Your host, Katie Kaspari, has what it takes to restore it and seed the very core of long-term human success and happiness. As a seasoned marketer with over 20 years of experience as a CMO in the energy sector, and the founder and CEO of a successful coaching practice in the heart of London, she knows exactly what it takes to be UNSHAKEABLE and have it all – career, relationships, and happiness!

title: Smoke & Stories: Inside The Smoking Gun
author: george wilson
description: Smoke & Stories: Inside The Smoking Gun is a lifestyle and hospitality podcast inspired by the bold energy, Southern comfort, and late-night atmosphere of The Smoking Gun in San Diego. Each episode explores the personality behind the venue: the food, the drinks, the guest experience, the team culture, and the unique moments that make a great bar and restaurant memorable. The show is designed to celebrate conversation, creativity, and community while giving listeners a feel for the warm, lively spirit that defines the brand.At the center of the series is a simple idea: every great night out has a story worth sharing. Whether the conversation is about signature cocktails, crowd-favorite dishes, game-day energy, or the art of hospitality, the podcast brings those stories to life in a way that feels inviting and entertaining. Listeners can visit here to connect the experience with the official presence of The Smoking Gun and discover what makes the venue stand out.The objective of the series is to strengthen brand identity, attract new guests, and create a deeper emotional connection with the audience. It also positions The Smoking Gun as more than a destination; it becomes a voice in the conversation around food, drinks, nightlife, and Southern-inspired dining culture.

title: Ideas that have shaped human history as expressed in poetry, speeches, myth and documents
author: Allan Anforth
description:
This podcast program is my personal effort to address ideas that have shaped human history as expressed in poetry, speeches, myths and documents. The programs will be posted weekly or more frequently depending on their content and the work involved


Mostly, but not always, the topics deal with what some people might describe as serious issues, which some might find depressing, unduly taxing on their concentration or offensive to their present world view. Such people are probably better to give this program a miss.

 

The program necessarily addresses existential, political, historical, philosophical, sociological and scientific issues of my choosing and no doubt, loaded with my personal biases. The purpose of is to encourage or facilitate people to think about the issues presented, for which I offer no apology for those who are offended.


Some of the literatures (poems, speeches, myths and documents) are also very long, which makes the oral presentation difficult and causes errors. It also can tax the concentration and interest of readers. For that reason, I have sometimes read only part of the relevant literature and summarised the content of the excised parts.

title: UNI LIFE WITH OLIVE
author: Olive
description: unilife with olive, is a show that explain how university runs in real life not movies this channel is interested in uni and also life outside school

title: Publishing Pathway: Find your spot on the publishing map
author: Colleen McCubbin
description: Book Hatchery Campus Radio is a window where you'll overhear conversations from our author community. We gather writers & help them move from uncertainty and isolation to confident authorship, so they can bring meaningful work to readers. Stop wondering how to publish your book and start walking on your publishing pathway.

title: Joshua’s Generation Podcast
author: Joshua's Generation
description: The Joshua’s Generation Podcast with Apostle Joshua Iyemifokhae features biblical teachings, sermons, prayer messages, and discipleship content designed to strengthen your faith, deepen your relationship with Jesus Christ, and equip you for victorious Christian living. New episodes are released regularly.

title: Tender Space
author: Confidence
description:
Tender Space is a soft place for honest conversations about life, mental health, fading friendships, emotional exhaustion, healing, freedom, and the quiet struggles we carry alone.
This podcast explores the parts of being human we don’t always know how to explain — overthinking at night, feeling lost in life, outgrowing people, trying to heal while still hurting, and learning how to exist in a world that can feel both beautiful and unfair.
Through deep reflections, real conversations, and vulnerable storytelling, Tender Space reminds listeners that they are not alone in what they feel.

title: Legacy Dads with Dave and Dante
author: Legacy Dads
description: Real men with authentic faith. Legacy Dads is a community of Christian men focusing on issues such as marriage, parenting, and leadership. We equip men who are trying to balance faith, our modern lives, and leading our families to a lasting legacy.

title: Hear Me Out
author: TROY Public Radio
description:
Host Reana Wallace is bringing us together through the interests we don’t have in common.

Sharing the things we love with the people we love, this is Hear Me Out!

title: Talking Social Change
author: HoneyVic Productions.
description:
Talking Social Change is a platform for underrepresented voices and ideas that need to be heard. We have honest conversations about the social issues that matter — talks built on mutual respect, real perspective, and honesty, with a single aim: turning open dialogue into real change.
Talking Social Change is produced by HoneyVic Productions, a nonprofit that believes every community deserves a stage — using theatre, performance, and education to lift up unheard voices and turn dialogue into meaningful action.

title: Frum Our View Podcast
author: The Gzng
description: Welcome to Frum Our View podcast where we bring you sports and real life conversation from a fans perspective

title: ON TASTE. with Alexandra Wakim and Alli DelGrippo
author: Dearest Media
description: A podcast about the intersection of fashion & wine from stylist Alexandra Wakim and wine pro Alli DelGrippo.

title: Guess You Had To Be There
author: Lee Serviss
description:
Lee Serviss lives just outside Ottawa, Ontario with his wife and daughter. By day, he works as a carpenter for a local home builder. Off the job, he's spent most of his life chasing creative pursuits—from writing songs and fronting bands to performing stand-up comedy and hosting podcasts.
On Guess You Had to Be There, Lee shares stories from everyday life, explores the strange thoughts that keep him entertained, and occasionally disappears down a thought-provoking rabbit hole. It's part storytelling, part comedy, and part figuring life out as he goes.

title: Sierra Leone Community Friday Sermons (USA)
author: Sesay
description:
Assalamu alaykum, habibi and habibati,
Our aim is to stay connected through the Friday khutbahs from across Sierra Leonean communities here in the USA. With the permission of the khateebs and masjid leadership, we hope these recordings benefit all who listen.
"When the call is proclaimed for Jumu'ah prayer, come to the remembrance of Allah and leave trade. That is better for you, if you only knew." — Qur'an 62:9 insha'Allah, we will update it weekly.

title: Ayvenwood: Immersive Sleep Stories for Kids
author: Jackie Nelson
description:
Between the day and the night, there is a place called Ayvenwood... An ancient, enchanted forest that inspires wonder and belonging.  Each episode is an immersive sleep story designed for older children (9+) and the families who love them. Listeners are invited to return to Ayvenwood again and again whenever they need rest, comfort, or a quiet place to fall asleep. 


Through original storytelling rooted in gratitude, Ayvenwood offers a nightly return to the kind of joy that never really leaves us. Created and narrated by Jackie Nelson. New episodes released regularly.


Wherever you are, Ayvenwood is only a breath away.

title: Sozo Firm Insights
author: Andrew Jensen
description: Strategic growth & leadership insights from Sozo Firm for businesses, nonprofit organizations, government agencies and higher education institutions.

title: Killing Time: A Horror Movie Podcast
author: Aubrey Grindstaff
description: Everything for all Scream Queens and Horror Fiends.  Hosted by Aubrey Grindstaff.

title: The Reveal Podcast with Elle Chase
author: Elle Chase
description:
What if the questions we're afraid to ask… are the ones that set us free?
I'm Elle Chase—coach, author, and sexologist—and this is The Reveal, a podcast about the intimate, often overlooked spaces where sex, identity, and emotional truth meet.
Each week, I talk with experts, thinkers, and storytellers to explore what it means to feel more whole, more seen, and more free.
No shame. No performance. Just open honest conversations about being human - because, the things we hide, often hold the most truth.
Welcome to The Reveal.

title: Now Why Would you Say That?!
author: Kandy
description: Just a Black mom, nurse, friend, veteran, late in life lesbian and educator in perimenopause living out lout!

title: On The Mark
author: West Australian Football League
description: Football broadcasting legend Mark Readings sits down with some of the biggest names across the WAFL and WAFLW competitions for candid and in-depth chats about their stories and life outside of footy.

title: Foul Play: Crime Series
author: Shane L. Waters, Wendy Cee, Gemma Hoskins
description: Welcome to Foul Play: Crime Series, the riveting true crime podcast that takes you on a deep dive into the world's most gripping cases. Each season unravels a unique case—choose one that intrigues you and dive in.

title: The Still Mind with Wendi Renay
author: Wendi Renay
description:
Wendi Renay is a creator, speaker, and mental-wellness educator who blends psychology, identity work, and grounded storytelling to help people find clarity and rebuild self-trust. She began her online presence under the brand DevOUT in 2022, supporting those healing from high-control religious systems. 
As her audience evolved, Wendi expanded into The Still Mind, a psychology-forward podcast focusing on executive function, emotional regulation, and identity reinvention. She also shares educational content on her YouTube channel at youtube.com/wendirenay.

title: Game Changers: The Future of Play
author: NOHOLD Inc.
description: Executive-level conversations about the business of games and AI — brought to you by leaders in AI and gaming.

title: Resettl - Career and Life in The Netherlands
author: Mirjam and Corine - Resettl
description:
Working in the Netherlands and wondering why nobody warned you it would be this different?
Resettl is the podcast for international professionals finding their footing in Dutch work and life. We're Mirjam and Corine, career coaches and former expats. Our backgrounds are in HR and marketing, so we know how Dutch employers really decide, and how to position you so the right people notice your value.
We share insights and real examples from our coaching practice: the Dutch job market, Dutch (work)place culture (yes, they're very direct), and building a life that genuinely fits.

title: Sacred Vows, Dirty Money
author: Dr. Rome
description: Original short-form audio drama. The Sounds of Seduction, Suspense, and Storytelling. Where secrets come dressed for the occasion.  Every chapter reveals the complexities of our member billionaire's and power broker's lives full of revenge, betrayal, survival, romance, power, faith, as clarity arrives from the last direction anyone expected, narrated by "Shadow". New chapters every Wednesday and your first two chapters are free. No coins, No ads, follow at home, in the gym, while commuting, during lunch and share with friends, but do not miss a chapter. Pour a glass of your favorite hot or cold beverage, your seat awaits you in The Velvet Parlour, where the Parlour is always listening. TheVelvetParlour.VIP

title: JoinIn
author: Juniper Education
description:
JoinIn is Juniper Education’s podcast, hosted by CEO Sarah Barton, helping school and trust leaders navigate key developments in education. 
Each episode explores policies, challenges and innovations from SEND and attendance to leadership, data and AI; featuring expert insights and real-world experience. 
Designed for education professionals, JoinIn keeps you informed, connects critical issues and supports confident decision-making in a rapidly changing landscape.
